kjv.1CH_25_3: Of Jeduthun: the sons of Jeduthun; Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the hands of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with a harp, to give thanks and to praise the Lord.
kjv.1CH_25_4: Of Heman: the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamtiezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth:
kjv.1CH_25_5: All these were the sons of Heman the king's seer in the words of God, to lift up the horn. And God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
kjv.1CH_25_6: All these were under the hands of their father for song in the house of the Lord, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps, for the service of the house of God, according to the king's order to Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman.
kjv.1CH_25_7: So the number of them, with their brethren that were instructed in the songs of the Lord, even all that were cunning, was two hundred fourscore and eight.
kjv.1CH_25_8: And they cast lots, ward against ward, as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholar.
kjv.1CH_25_9: Now the first lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph: the second to Gedaliah, who with his brethren and sons were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_10: The third to Zaccur, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_11: The fourth to Izri, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_12: The fifth to Nethaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_13: The sixth to Bukkiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_14: The seventh to Jesharelah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_15: The eighth to Jeshaiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_16: The ninth to Mattaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_17: The tenth to Shimei, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_18: The eleventh to Azareel, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_19: The twelfth to Hashabiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_20: The thirteenth to Shubael, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_21: The fourteenth to Mattithiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_22: The fifteenth to Jeremoth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_23: The sixteenth to Hananiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_24: The seventeenth to Joshbekashah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_25: The eighteenth to Hanani, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_26: The nineteenth to Mallothi, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_27: The twentieth to Eliathah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_28: The one and twentieth to Hothir, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_29: The two and twentieth to Giddalti, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_30: The three and twentieth to Mahazioth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
kjv.1CH_25_31: The four and twentieth to Romamtiezer, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve.
kjv.1CH_26_1: Concerning the divisions of the porters: Of the Korhites was Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
kjv.1CH_26_2: And the sons of Meshelemiah were, Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
kjv.1CH_26_3: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.
kjv.1CH_26_4: Moreover the sons of Obededom were, Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth.
kjv.1CH_26_5: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth: for God blessed him.
kjv.1CH_26_6: Also unto Shemaiah his son were sons born, that ruled throughout the house of their father: for they were mighty men of valour.
kjv.1CH_26_7: The sons of Shemaiah; Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were strong men, Elihu, and Semachiah.
kjv.1CH_26_8: All these of the sons of Obededom: they and their sons and their brethren, able men for strength for the service, were threescore and two of Obededom.
kjv.1CH_26_9: And Meshelemiah had sons and brethren, strong men, eighteen.
kjv.1CH_26_10: Also Hosah, of the children of Merari, had sons; Simri the chief, (for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father made him the chief;)
kjv.1CH_26_11: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth: all the sons and brethren of Hosah were thirteen.
kjv.1CH_26_12: Among these were the divisions of the porters, even among the chief men, having wards one against another, to minister in the house of the Lord.
kjv.1CH_26_13: And they cast lots, as well the small as the great, according to the house of their fathers, for every gate.
kjv.1CH_26_14: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. Then for Zechariah his son, a wise counsellor, they cast lots; and his lot came out northward.
kjv.1CH_26_15: To Obededom southward; and to his sons the house of Asuppim.
kjv.1CH_26_16: To Shuppim and Hosah the lot came forth westward, with the gate Shallecheth, by the causeway of the going up, ward against ward.
kjv.1CH_26_17: Eastward were six Levites, northward four a day, southward four a day, and toward Asuppim two and two.
kjv.1CH_26_18: At Parbar westward, four at the causeway, and two at Parbar.
kjv.1CH_26_19: These are the divisions of the porters among the sons of Kore, and among the sons of Merari.
kjv.1CH_26_20: And of the Levites, Ahijah was over the treasures of the house of God, and over the treasures of the dedicated things.
kjv.1CH_26_21: As concerning the sons of Laadan; the sons of the Gershonite Laadan, chief fathers, even of Laadan the Gershonite, were Jehieli.
kjv.1CH_26_22: The sons of Jehieli; Zetham, and Joel his brother, which were over the treasures of the house of the Lord.
kjv.1CH_26_23: Of the Amramites, and the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
kjv.1CH_26_24: And Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was ruler of the treasures.
kjv.1CH_26_25: And his brethren by Eliezer; Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.
kjv.1CH_26_26: Which Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which David the king, and the chief fathers, the captains over thousands and hundreds, and the captains of the host, had dedicated.
kjv.1CH_26_27: Out of the spoils won in battles did they dedicate to maintain the house of the Lord.
kjv.1CH_26_28: And all that Samuel the seer, and Saul the son of Kish, and Abner the son of Ner, and Joab the son of Zeruiah, had dedicated; and whosoever had dedicated any thing, it was under the hand of Shelomith, and of his brethren.
kjv.1CH_26_29: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were for the outward business over Israel, for officers and judges.
kjv.1CH_26_30: And of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seven hundred, were officers among them of Israel on this side Jordan westward in all the business of the Lord, and in the service of the king.
kjv.1CH_26_31: Among the Hebronites was Jerijah the chief, even among the Hebronites, according to the generations of his fathers. In the fortieth year of the reign of David they were sought for, and there were found among them mighty men of valour at Jazer of Gilead.
kjv.1CH_26_32: And his brethren, men of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred chief fathers, whom king David made rulers over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh, for every matter pertaining to God, and affairs of the king.
kjv.1CH_27_1: Now the children of Israel after their number, to wit, the chief fathers and captains of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in any matter of the courses, which came in and went out month by month throughout all the months of the year, of every course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_2: Over the first course for the first month was Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_3: Of the children of Perez was the chief of all the captains of the host for the first month.
kjv.1CH_27_4: And over the course of the second month was Dodai an Ahohite, and of his course was Mikloth also the ruler: in his course likewise were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_5: The third captain of the host for the third month was Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a chief priest: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_6: This is that Benaiah, who was mighty among the thirty, and above the thirty: and in his course was Ammizabad his son.
kjv.1CH_27_7: The fourth captain for the fourth month was Asahel the brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son after him: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_8: The fifth captain for the fifth month was Shamhuth the Izrahite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_9: The sixth captain for the sixth month was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_10: The seventh captain for the seventh month was Helez the Pelonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_11: The eighth captain for the eighth month was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_12: The ninth captain for the ninth month was Abiezer the Anetothite, of the Benjamites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_13: The tenth captain for the tenth month was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_14: The eleventh captain for the eleventh month was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_15: The twelfth captain for the twelfth month was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
kjv.1CH_27_16: Furthermore over the tribes of Israel: the ruler of the Reubenites was Eliezer the son of Zichri: of the Simeonites, Shephatiah the son of Maachah:
kjv.1CH_27_17: Of the Levites, Hashabiah the son of Kemuel: of the Aaronites, Zadok:
kjv.1CH_27_18: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David: of Issachar, Omri the son of Michael:
kjv.1CH_27_19: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah: of Naphtali, Jerimoth the son of Azriel:
kjv.1CH_27_20: Of the children of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Azaziah: of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah:
kjv.1CH_27_21: Of the half tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo the son of Zechariah: of Benjamin, Jaasiel the son of Abner:
kjv.1CH_27_22: Of Dan, Azareel the son of Jeroham. These were the princes of the tribes of Israel.
kjv.1CH_27_23: But David took not the number of them from twenty years old and under: because the Lord had said he would increase Israel like to the stars of the heavens.
kjv.1CH_27_24: Joab the son of Zeruiah began to number, but he finished not, because there fell wrath for it against Israel; neither was the number put in the account of the chronicles of king David.
kjv.1CH_27_25: And over the king's treasures was Azmaveth the son of Adiel: and over the storehouses in the fields, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the castles, was Jehonathan the son of Uzziah:
kjv.1CH_27_26: And over them that did the work of the field for tillage of the ground was Ezri the son of Chelub:
kjv.1CH_27_27: And over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite: over the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite:
kjv.1CH_27_28: And over the olive trees and the sycomore trees that were in the low plains was Baalhanan the Gederite: and over the cellars of oil was Joash:
kjv.1CH_27_29: And over the herds that fed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite: and over the herds that were in the valleys was Shaphat the son of Adlai:
kjv.1CH_27_30: Over the camels also was Obil the Ishmaelite: and over the asses was Jehdeiah the Meronothite:
kjv.1CH_27_31: And over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagerite. All these were the rulers of the substance which was king David's.
kjv.1CH_27_32: Also Jonathan David's uncle was a counsellor, a wise man, and a scribe: and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni was with the king's sons:
kjv.1CH_27_33: And Ahithophel was the king's counsellor: and Hushai the Archite was the king's companion:
kjv.1CH_27_34: And after Ahithophel was Jehoiada the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar: and the general of the king's army was Joab.
kjv.1CH_28_1: And David assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, and the captains of the companies that ministered to the king by course, and the captains over the thousands, and captains over the hundreds, and the stewards over all the substance and possession of the king, and of his sons, with the officers, and with the mighty men, and with all the valiant men, unto Jerusalem.
kjv.1CH_28_2: Then David the king stood up upon his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren, and my people: As for me, I had in mine heart to build an house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and for the footstool of our God, and had made ready for the building:
kjv.1CH_28_3: But God said unto me, Thou shalt not build an house for my name, because thou hast been a man of war, and hast shed blood.
kjv.1CH_28_4: Howbeit the Lord God of Israel chose me before all the house of my father to be king over Israel for ever: for he hath chosen Judah to be the ruler; and of the house of Judah, the house of my father; and among the sons of my father he liked me to make me king over all Israel:
kjv.1CH_28_5: And of all my sons, (for the Lord hath given me many sons,) he hath chosen Solomon my son to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of the Lord over Israel.
kjv.1CH_28_6: And he said unto me, Solomon thy son, he shall build my house and my courts: for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.
kjv.1CH_28_7: Moreover I will establish his kingdom for ever, if he be constant to do my commandments and my judgments, as at this day.
kjv.1CH_28_8: Now therefore in the sight of all Israel the congregation of the Lord, and in the audience of our God, keep and seek for all the commandments of the Lord your God: that ye may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance for your children after you for ever.
kjv.1CH_28_9: And thou, Solomon my son, know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing mind: for the Lord searcheth all hearts, and understandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts: if thou seek him, he will be found of thee; but if thou forsake him, he will cast thee off for ever.
kjv.1CH_28_10: Take heed now; for the Lord hath chosen thee to build an house for the sanctuary: be strong, and do it.
kjv.1CH_28_11: Then David gave to Solomon his son the pattern of the porch, and of the houses thereof, and of the treasuries thereof, and of the upper chambers thereof, and of the inner parlours thereof, and of the place of the mercy seat,
kjv.1CH_28_12: And the pattern of all that he had by the spirit, of the courts of the house of the Lord, and of all the chambers round about, of the treasuries of the house of God, and of the treasuries of the dedicated things:
kjv.1CH_28_13: Also for the courses of the priests and the Levites, and for all the work of the service of the house of the Lord, and for all the vessels of service in the house of the Lord.
kjv.1CH_28_14: He gave of gold by weight for things of gold, for all instruments of all manner of service; silver also for all instruments of silver by weight, for all instruments of every kind of service:
kjv.1CH_28_15: Even the weight for the candlesticks of gold, and for their lamps of gold, by weight for every candlestick, and for the lamps thereof: and for the candlesticks of silver by weight, both for the candlestick, and also for the lamps thereof, according to the use of every candlestick.
kjv.1CH_28_16: And by weight he gave gold for the tables of shewbread, for every table; and likewise silver for the tables of silver:
kjv.1CH_28_17: Also pure gold for the fleshhooks, and the bowls, and the cups: and for the golden basons he gave gold by weight for every bason; and likewise silver by weight for every bason of silver:
kjv.1CH_28_18: And for the altar of incense refined gold by weight; and gold for the pattern of the chariot of the cherubims, that spread out their wings, and covered the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
kjv.1CH_28_19: All this, said David, the Lord made me understand in writing by his hand upon me, even all the works of this pattern.
kjv.1CH_28_20: And David said to Solomon his son, Be strong and of good courage, and do it: fear not, nor be dismayed: for the Lord God, even my God, will be with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee, until thou hast finished all the work for the service of the house of the Lord.
kjv.1CH_28_21: And, behold, the courses of the priests and the Levites, even they shall be with thee for all the service of the house of God: and there shall be with thee for all manner of workmanship every willing skilful man, for any manner of service: also the princes and all the people will be wholly at thy commandment.
kjv.1CH_29_1: Furthermore David the king said unto all the congregation, Solomon my son, whom alone God hath chosen, is yet young and tender, and the work is great: for the palace is not for man, but for the Lord God.
kjv.1CH_29_2: Now I have prepared with all my might for the house of my God the gold for things to be made of gold, and the silver for things of silver, and the brass for things of brass, the iron for things of iron, and wood for things of wood; onyx stones, and stones to be set, glistering stones, and of divers colours, and all manner of precious stones, and marble stones in abundance.
kjv.1CH_29_3: Moreover, because I have set my affection to the house of my God, I have of mine own proper good, of gold and silver, which I have given to the house of my God, over and above all that I have prepared for the holy house.
kjv.1CH_29_4: Even three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the houses withal:
kjv.1CH_29_5: The gold for things of gold, and the silver for things of silver, and for all manner of work to be made by the hands of artificers. And who then is willing to consecrate his service this day unto the Lord?
kjv.1CH_29_6: Then the chief of the fathers and princes of the tribes of Israel and the captains of thousands and of hundreds, with the rulers of the king's work, offered willingly,
kjv.1CH_29_7: And gave for the service of the house of God of gold five thousand talents and ten thousand drams, and of silver ten thousand talents, and of brass eighteen thousand talents, and one hundred thousand talents of iron.
kjv.1CH_29_8: And they with whom precious stones were found gave them to the treasure of the house of the Lord, by the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.
kjv.1CH_29_9: Then the people rejoiced, for that they offered willingly, because with perfect heart they offered willingly to the Lord: and David the king also rejoiced with great joy.
kjv.1CH_29_10: Wherefore David blessed the Lord before all the congregation: and David said, Blessed be thou, Lord God of Israel our father, for ever and ever.
kjv.1CH_29_11: Thine, O Lord is the greatness, and the power, and the glory, and the victory, and the majesty: for all that is in the heaven and in the earth is thine; thine is the kingdom, O Lord, and thou art exalted as head above all.
kjv.1CH_29_12: Both riches and honour come of thee, and thou reignest over all; and in thine hand is power and might; and in thine hand it is to make great, and to give strength unto all.
kjv.1CH_29_13: Now therefore, our God, we thank thee, and praise thy glorious name.
kjv.1CH_29_14: But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to offer so willingly after this sort? for all things come of thee, and of thine own have we given thee.
kjv.1CH_29_15: For we are strangers before thee, and sojourners, as were all our fathers: our days on the earth are as a shadow, and there is none abiding.
kjv.1CH_29_16: O Lord our God, all this store that we have prepared to build thee an house for thine holy name cometh of thine hand, and is all thine own.
kjv.1CH_29_17: I know also, my God, that thou triest the heart, and hast pleasure in uprightness. As for me, in the uprightness of mine heart I have willingly offered all these things: and now have I seen with joy thy people, which are present here, to offer willingly unto thee.
kjv.1CH_29_18: O Lord God of Abraham, Isaac, and of Israel, our fathers, keep this for ever in the imagination of the thoughts of the heart of thy people, and prepare their heart unto thee:
kjv.1CH_29_19: And give unto Solomon my son a perfect heart, to keep thy commandments, thy testimonies, and thy statutes, and to do all these things, and to build the palace, for the which I have made provision.
kjv.1CH_29_20: And David said to all the congregation, Now bless the Lord your God. And all the congregation blessed the Lord God of their fathers, and bowed down their heads, and worshipped the Lord, and the king.
kjv.1CH_29_21: And they sacrificed sacrifices unto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings unto the Lord, on the morrow after that day, even a thousand bullocks, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel:
kjv.1CH_29_22: And did eat and drink before the Lord on that day with great gladness. And they made Solomon the son of David king the second time, and anointed him unto the Lord to be the chief governor, and Zadok to be priest.
kjv.1CH_29_23: Then Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord as king instead of David his father, and prospered; and all Israel obeyed him.
kjv.1CH_29_24: And all the princes, and the mighty men, and all the sons likewise of king David, submitted themselves unto Solomon the king.
kjv.1CH_29_25: And the Lord magnified Solomon exceedingly in the sight of all Israel, and bestowed upon him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Israel.
kjv.1CH_29_26: Thus David the son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
kjv.1CH_29_27: And the time that he reigned over Israel was forty years; seven years reigned he in Hebron, and thirty and three years reigned he in Jerusalem.
kjv.1CH_29_28: And he died in a good old age, full of days, riches, and honour: and Solomon his son reigned in his stead.
kjv.1CH_29_29: Now the acts of David the king, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of Samuel the seer, and in the book of Nathan the prophet, and in the book of Gad the seer,
kjv.1CH_29_30: With all his reign and his might, and the times that went over him, and over Israel, and over all the kingdoms of the countries.

net.1CH_1_4: Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Japheth’s Descendants
net.1CH_1_5: The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
net.1CH_1_6: The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
net.1CH_1_7: The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittites, and the Rodanites. Ham’s Descendants
net.1CH_1_8: The sons of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
net.1CH_1_9: The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
net.1CH_1_10: Cush was the father of Nimrod, who established himself as a mighty warrior on earth.
net.1CH_1_11: Mizraim was the father of the Ludites, Anamites, Lehabites, Naphtuhites,
net.1CH_1_12: Pathrusites, Casluhites (from whom the Philistines descended) , and the Caphtorites.
net.1CH_1_13: Canaan was the father of Sidon—his firstborn—and Heth,
net.1CH_1_14: as well as the Jebusites, Amorites, Girgashites,
net.1CH_1_16: Arvadites, Zemarites, and Hamathites. Shem’s Descendants
net.1CH_1_17: The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram. The sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.
net.1CH_1_18: Arphaxad was the father of Shelah, and Shelah was the father of Eber.
net.1CH_1_19: Two sons were born to Eber: the first was named Peleg, for during his lifetime the earth was divided; his brother’s name was Joktan.
net.1CH_1_20: Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
net.1CH_1_23: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were the sons of Joktan.
net.1CH_1_28: The sons of Abraham: Isaac and Ishmael.
net.1CH_1_29: These were their descendants: Ishmael’s Descendants Ishmael’s firstborn son was Nebaioth; the others were Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
net.1CH_1_31: Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These were the sons of Ishmael. Keturah’s Descendants
net.1CH_1_32: The sons to whom Keturah, Abraham’s concubine, gave birth: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, Shuah. The sons of Jokshan: Sheba and Dedan.
net.1CH_1_33: The sons of Midian: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the sons of Keturah. Isaac’s Descendants
net.1CH_1_34: Abraham was the father of Isaac. The sons of Isaac: Esau and Israel. Esau’s Descendants
net.1CH_1_35: The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
net.1CH_1_36: The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zephi, Gatam, Kenaz, and (by Timna) Amalek.
net.1CH_1_37: The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. The Descendants of Seir
net.1CH_1_38: The sons of Seir: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.
net.1CH_1_39: The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam. (Timna was Lotan’s sister.)
net.1CH_1_40: The sons of Shobal: Alyan, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam. The sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah.
net.1CH_1_41: The son of Anah: Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hamran, Eshban, Ithran, and Keran.
net.1CH_1_42: The sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, Jaakan. The sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran. Kings of Edom
net.1CH_1_43: These were the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king ruled over the Israelites: Bela son of Beor; the name of his city was Dinhabah.
net.1CH_1_44: When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah, succeeded him.
net.1CH_1_45: When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites succeeded him.
net.1CH_1_46: When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad succeeded him. He struck down the Midianites in the plains of Moab; the name of his city was Avith.
net.1CH_1_47: When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah succeeded him.
net.1CH_1_48: When Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the River succeeded him.
net.1CH_1_49: When Shaul died, Baal Hanan son of Achbor succeeded him.
net.1CH_1_50: When Baal Hanan died, Hadad succeeded him; the name of his city was Pai. His wife was Mehetabel, daughter of Matred, daughter of Me-Zahab.
net.1CH_1_51: Hadad died. Tribal Chiefs of Edom The tribal chiefs of Edom were: Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
net.1CH_1_54: Magdiel, and Iram. These were the tribal chiefs of Edom.
net.1CH_2_1: These were the sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah; Issachar and Zebulun;
net.1CH_2_2: Dan, Joseph, and Benjamin; Naphtali, Gad, and Asher. Judah’s Descendants
net.1CH_2_3: The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, and Shelah. These three were born to him by Bathshua, a Canaanite woman. Er, Judah’s firstborn, displeased the Lord, so the Lord killed him.
net.1CH_2_4: Tamar, Judah’s daughter-in-law, bore to him Perez and Zerah. Judah had five sons in all.
net.1CH_2_5: The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.
net.1CH_2_6: The sons of Zerah: Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Kalkol, Dara —five in all.
net.1CH_2_7: The son of Carmi: Achan, who brought the disaster on Israel when he stole what was devoted to God.
net.1CH_2_9: The sons born to Hezron: Jerahmeel, Ram, and Caleb. Ram’s Descendants
net.1CH_2_10: Ram was the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, the tribal chief of Judah.
net.1CH_2_11: Nahshon was the father of Salma, and Salma was the father of Boaz.
net.1CH_2_12: Boaz was the father of Obed, and Obed was the father of Jesse.
net.1CH_2_13: Jesse was the father of Eliab, his firstborn; Abinadab was born second, Shimea third,
net.1CH_2_16: Their sisters were Zeruiah and Abigail. Zeruiah’s three sons were Abshai, Joab, and Asahel.
net.1CH_2_17: Abigail bore Amasa, whose father was Jether the Ishmaelite. Caleb’s Descendants
net.1CH_2_18: Caleb son of Hezron fathered sons by his wife Azubah (also known as Jerioth). Her sons were Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
net.1CH_2_19: When Azubah died, Caleb married Ephrath, who bore him Hur.
net.1CH_2_20: Hur was the father of Uri, and Uri was the father of Bezalel.
net.1CH_2_21: Later Hezron slept with the daughter of Makir, the father of Gilead. (He had married her when he was sixty years old.) She bore him Segub.
net.1CH_2_22: Segub was the father of Jair, who owned twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead.
net.1CH_2_23: (Geshur and Aram captured the towns of Jair, along with Kenath and its sixty surrounding towns.) All these were descendants of Makir, the father of Gilead.
net.1CH_2_24: After Hezron’s death, Caleb slept with Ephrath, his father Hezron’s widow, and she bore to him Ashhur the father of Tekoa. Jerahmeel’s Descendants
net.1CH_2_25: The sons of Jerahmeel, Hezron’s firstborn, were Ram, the firstborn, Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah.
net.1CH_2_26: Jerahmeel had another wife named Atarah; she was Onam’s mother.
net.1CH_2_27: The sons of Ram, Jerahmeel’s firstborn, were Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.
net.1CH_2_28: The sons of Onam were Shammai and Jada. The sons of Shammai: Nadab and Abishur.
net.1CH_2_29: Abishur’s wife was Abihail, who bore him Ahban and Molid.
net.1CH_2_30: The sons of Nadab: Seled and Appaim. (Seled died without having sons.)
net.1CH_2_31: The son of Appaim: Ishi. The son of Ishi: Sheshan. The son of Sheshan: Ahlai.
net.1CH_2_32: The sons of Jada, Shammai’s brother: Jether and Jonathan. (Jether died without having sons.)
net.1CH_2_33: The sons of Jonathan: Peleth and Zaza. These were the descendants of Jerahmeel.
net.1CH_2_34: Sheshan had no sons, only daughters. Sheshan had an Egyptian servant named Jarha.
net.1CH_2_35: Sheshan gave his daughter to his servant Jarha as a wife; she bore him Attai.
net.1CH_2_36: Attai was the father of Nathan, and Nathan was the father of Zabad.
net.1CH_2_37: Zabad was the father of Ephlal, and Ephlal was the father of Obed.
net.1CH_2_38: Obed was the father of Jehu, and Jehu was the father of Azariah.
net.1CH_2_39: Azariah was the father of Helez, and Helez was the father of Eleasah.
net.1CH_2_40: Eleasah was the father of Sismai, and Sismai was the father of Shallum.
net.1CH_2_41: Shallum was the father of Jekamiah, and Jekamiah was the father of Elishama. More of Caleb’s Descendants
net.1CH_2_42: The sons of Caleb, Jerahmeel’s brother: His firstborn Mesha, the father of Ziph, and his second son Mareshah, the father of Hebron.
net.1CH_2_43: The sons of Hebron: Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.
net.1CH_2_44: Shema was the father of Raham, the father of Jorkeam. Rekem was the father of Shammai.
net.1CH_2_45: Shammai’s son was Maon, who was the father of Beth Zur.
net.1CH_2_46: Caleb’s concubine Ephah bore Haran, Moza, and Gazez. Haran was the father of Gazez.
net.1CH_2_47: The sons of Jahdai: Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.
net.1CH_2_48: Caleb’s concubine Maacah bore Sheber and Tirhanah.
net.1CH_2_49: She also bore Shaaph the father of Madmannah and Sheva the father of Machbenah and Gibea. Caleb’s daughter was Achsah.
net.1CH_2_50: These were the descendants of Caleb. The sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrath: Shobal, the father of Kiriath Jearim,
net.1CH_2_51: Salma, the father of Bethlehem, and Hareph, the father of Beth Gader.
net.1CH_2_52: The sons of Shobal, the father of Kiriath Jearim, were Haroeh, half the Manahathites,
net.1CH_2_53: the clans of Kiriath Jearim—the Ithrites, Puthites, Shumathites, and Mishraites. (The Zorathites and Eshtaolites descended from these groups.)
net.1CH_2_54: The sons of Salma: Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atroth Beth Joab, half the Manahathites, the Zorites,
net.1CH_2_55: and the clans of the scribes who lived in Jabez: the Tirathites, Shimeathites, and Sucathites. These are the Kenites who descended from Hammath, the father of Beth Rechab.
net.1CH_3_1: These were the sons of David who were born to him in Hebron: The firstborn was Amnon, whose mother was Ahinoam from Jezreel; the second was Daniel, whose mother was Abigail from Carmel;
net.1CH_3_2: the third was Absalom whose mother was Maacah, daughter of King Talmai of Geshur; the fourth was Adonijah, whose mother was Haggith;
net.1CH_3_3: the fifth was Shephatiah, whose mother was Abital; the sixth was Ithream, whose mother was Eglah, David’s wife.
net.1CH_3_4: These six were born to David in Hebron, where he ruled for seven years and six months. He ruled thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_3_5: These were the sons born to him in Jerusalem: Shimea, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon—the mother of these four was Bathsheba the daughter of Ammiel.
net.1CH_3_6: The other nine were Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
net.1CH_3_9: These were all the sons of David, not counting the sons of his concubines. Tamar was their sister. Solomon’s Descendants
net.1CH_3_10: Solomon’s son was Rehoboam, followed by Abijah his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son,
net.1CH_3_11: Joram his son, Ahaziah his son, Joash his son,
net.1CH_3_12: Amaziah his son, Azariah his son, Jotham his son,
net.1CH_3_13: Ahaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manasseh his son,
net.1CH_3_14: Amon his son, Josiah his son.
net.1CH_3_15: The sons of Josiah: Johanan was the firstborn; Jehoiakim was born second; Zedekiah third; and Shallum fourth.
net.1CH_3_16: The sons of Jehoiakim: his son Jehoiachin and his son Zedekiah.
net.1CH_3_17: The sons of Jehoiachin the exile: Shealtiel his son,
net.1CH_3_18: Malkiram, Pedaiah, Shenazzar, Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.
net.1CH_3_19: The sons of Pedaiah: Zerubbabel and Shimei. The sons of Zerubbabel: Meshullam and Hananiah. Shelomith was their sister.
net.1CH_3_20: The five others were Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab Hesed.
net.1CH_3_21: The descendants of Hananiah: Pelatiah, Jeshaiah, the sons of Rephaiah, of Arnan, of Obadiah, and of Shecaniah.
net.1CH_3_22: The descendants of Shecaniah: Shemaiah and his sons: Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat—six in all.
net.1CH_3_23: The sons of Neariah: Elioenai, Hizkiah, and Azrikam—three in all.
net.1CH_3_24: The sons of Elioenai: Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Delaiah, and Anani—seven in all.
net.1CH_4_1: The descendants of Judah: Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.
net.1CH_4_2: Reaiah the son of Shobal was the father of Jahath, and Jahath was the father of Ahumai and Lahad. These were the clans of the Zorathites.
net.1CH_4_3: These were the sons of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash. Their sister was Hazzelelponi.
net.1CH_4_4: Penuel was the father of Gedor, and Ezer was the father of Hushah. These were the descendants of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah and the father of Bethlehem.
net.1CH_4_5: Ashhur the father of Tekoa had two wives, Helah and Naarah.
net.1CH_4_6: Naarah bore him Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari. These were the sons of Naarah.
net.1CH_4_7: The sons of Helah: Zereth, Zohar, Ethnan,
net.1CH_4_8: and Koz, who was the father of Anub, Hazzobebah, and the clans of Aharhel the son of Harum.
net.1CH_4_9: Jabez was more respected than his brothers. His mother had named him Jabez, for she said, “I experienced pain when I gave birth to him.”
net.1CH_4_10: Jabez called out to the God of Israel, “If only you would greatly bless me and expand my territory. May your hand be with me! Keep me from harm so I might not endure pain.” God answered his prayer.
net.1CH_4_11: Kelub, the brother of Shuhah, was the father of Mehir, who was the father of Eshton.
net.1CH_4_12: Eshton was the father of Beth Rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah, the father of Ir Nahash. These were the men of Recah.
net.1CH_4_13: The sons of Kenaz: Othniel and Seraiah. The sons of Othniel: Hathath and Meonothai.
net.1CH_4_14: Meonothai was the father of Ophrah. Seraiah was the father of Joab, the father of those who live in the Valley of the Craftsmen, for they were craftsmen.
net.1CH_4_15: The sons of Caleb son of Jephunneh: Iru, Elah, and Naam. The son of Elah: Kenaz.
net.1CH_4_16: The sons of Jehallelel: Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.
net.1CH_4_17: The sons of Ezrah: Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon. Mered’s wife Bithiah gave birth to Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah, the father of Eshtemoa.
net.1CH_4_18: (His Judahite wife gave birth to Jered the father of Gedor, Heber the father of Soco, and Jekuthiel the father of Zanoah.) These were the sons of Pharaoh’s daughter Bithiah, whom Mered married.
net.1CH_4_19: The sons of Hodiah’s wife, the sister of Naham: the father of Keilah the Garmite, and Eshtemoa the Maacathite.
net.1CH_4_20: The sons of Shimon: Amnon, Rinnah, Ben Hanan, and Tilon. The descendants of Ishi: Zoheth and Ben Zoheth.
net.1CH_4_21: The sons of Shelah son of Judah: Er the father of Lecah, Laadah the father of Mareshah, the clans of the linen workers at Beth Ashbea,
net.1CH_4_22: Jokim, the men of Cozeba, and Joash and Saraph, both of whom ruled in Moab and Jashubi Lehem. (This information is from ancient records.)
net.1CH_4_23: They were the potters who lived in Netaim and Gederah; they lived there and worked for the king. Simeon’s Descendants
net.1CH_4_24: The descendants of Simeon: Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Shaul,
net.1CH_4_25: his son Shallum, his son Mibsam, and his son Mishma.
net.1CH_4_26: The descendants of Mishma: his son Hammuel, his son Zaccur, and his son Shimei.
net.1CH_4_27: Shimei had sixteen sons and six daughters. But his brothers did not have many sons, so their whole clan was not as numerous as the sons of Judah.
net.1CH_4_28: They lived in Beer Sheba, Moladah, Hazar Shual,
net.1CH_4_31: Beth Marcaboth, Hazar Susim, Beth Biri, and Shaaraim. These were their towns until the reign of David.
net.1CH_4_32: Their settlements also included Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan—five towns,
net.1CH_4_33: along with all their settlements that surrounded these towns as far as Baal. These were the places where they lived; they kept genealogical records.
net.1CH_4_34: Their clan leaders were: Meshobab, Jamlech, Joshah son of Amaziah,
net.1CH_4_35: Joel, Jehu son of Joshibiah (son of Seraiah, son of Asiel),
net.1CH_4_36: Eleoenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
net.1CH_4_37: Ziza son of Shipi (son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah).
net.1CH_4_38: These who are named above were the leaders of their clans. Their extended families increased greatly in numbers.
net.1CH_4_39: They went to the entrance of Gedor, to the east of the valley, looking for pasture for their sheep.
net.1CH_4_40: They found fertile and rich pasture; the land was very broad, undisturbed and peaceful. Indeed some Hamites had been living there before that.
net.1CH_4_41: The men whose names are listed came during the time of King Hezekiah of Judah and attacked the Hamites’ settlements, as well as the Meunites they discovered there, and they wiped them out, as can be seen to this very day. They dispossessed them, for they found pasture for their sheep there.
net.1CH_4_42: Five hundred men of Simeon, led by Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi, went to the hill country of Seir
net.1CH_4_43: and defeated the rest of the Amalekite refugees; they live there to this very day.
net.1CH_5_1: The sons of Reuben, Israel’s firstborn— (Now he was the firstborn, but when he defiled his father’s bed, his rights as firstborn were given to the sons of Joseph, Israel’s son. So Reuben is not listed as firstborn in the genealogical records.
net.1CH_5_2: Though Judah was the strongest among his brothers and a leader descended from him, the right of the firstborn belonged to Joseph.)
net.1CH_5_3: The sons of Reuben, Israel’s firstborn: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
net.1CH_5_4: The descendants of Joel: his son Shemaiah, his son Gog, his son Shimei,
net.1CH_5_5: his son Micah, his son Reaiah, his son Baal,
net.1CH_5_6: and his son Beerah, whom King Tiglath-Pileser of Assyria carried into exile. Beerah was the tribal leader of Reuben.
net.1CH_5_7: His brothers by their clans, as listed in their genealogical records: The leader Jeiel, Zechariah,
net.1CH_5_8: and Bela son of Azaz, son of Shema, son of Joel. They lived in Aroer as far as Nebo and Baal Meon.
net.1CH_5_9: In the east they settled as far as the entrance to the wilderness that stretches to the Euphrates River, for their cattle had increased in numbers in the land of Gilead.
net.1CH_5_10: During the time of Saul they attacked the Hagrites and defeated them. They took over their territory in the entire eastern region of Gilead. Gad’s Descendants
net.1CH_5_11: The descendants of Gad lived near them in the land of Bashan, as far as Salecah.
net.1CH_5_12: They included Joel the leader, Shapham the second in command, Janai, and Shaphat in Bashan.
net.1CH_5_13: Their relatives, listed according to their families, included Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber—seven in all.
net.1CH_5_14: These were the sons of Abihail son of Huri, son of Jaroah, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jeshishai, son of Jahdo, son of Buz.
net.1CH_5_15: Ahi son of Abdiel, son of Guni, was the leader of the family.
net.1CH_5_16: They lived in Gilead, in Bashan and its surrounding settlements, and in the pasturelands of Sharon to their very borders.
net.1CH_5_17: All of them were listed in the genealogical records in the time of King Jotham of Judah and in the time of King Jeroboam of Israel.
net.1CH_5_18: The Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh had 44,760 men in their combined armies, warriors who carried shields and swords, were equipped with bows, and were trained for war.
net.1CH_5_19: They attacked the Hagrites, Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab.
net.1CH_5_20: They received divine help in fighting them, and the Hagrites and all their allies were handed over to them. They cried out to God during the battle; he responded to their prayers because they trusted in him.
net.1CH_5_21: They seized the Hagrites’ animals, including 50,000 camels, 250,000 sheep, and 2,000 donkeys. They also took captive 100,000 people.
net.1CH_5_22: Because God fought for them, they killed many of the enemy. They dispossessed the Hagrites and lived in their land until the exile. The Half-Tribe of Manasseh
net.1CH_5_23: The half-tribe of Manasseh settled in the land from Bashan as far as Baal Hermon, Senir, and Mount Hermon. They grew in number.
net.1CH_5_24: These were the leaders of their families: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel. They were skilled warriors, men of reputation, and leaders of their families.
net.1CH_5_25: But they were unfaithful to the God of their ancestors and worshiped instead the gods of the native peoples whom God had destroyed before them.
net.1CH_5_26: So the God of Israel stirred up King Pul of Assyria (that is, King Tiglath-Pileser of Assyria), and he carried away the Reubenites, Gadites, and half-tribe of Manasseh and took them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the river of Gozan, where they remain to this very day.
net.1CH_6_1: (5:27) The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
net.1CH_6_2: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
net.1CH_6_3: The children of Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
net.1CH_6_4: Eleazar was the father of Phinehas, and Phinehas was the father of Abishua.
net.1CH_6_5: Abishua was the father of Bukki, and Bukki was the father of Uzzi.
net.1CH_6_6: Uzzi was the father of Zerahiah, and Zerahiah was the father of Meraioth.
net.1CH_6_7: Meraioth was the father of Amariah, and Amariah was the father of Ahitub.
net.1CH_6_8: Ahitub was the father of Zadok, and Zadok was the father of Ahimaaz.
net.1CH_6_9: Ahimaaz was the father of Azariah, and Azariah was the father of Johanan.
net.1CH_6_10: Johanan was the father of Azariah, who served as a priest in the temple Solomon built in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_6_11: Azariah was the father of Amariah, and Amariah was the father of Ahitub.
net.1CH_6_12: Ahitub was the father of Zadok, and Zadok was the father of Shallum.
net.1CH_6_13: Shallum was the father of Hilkiah, and Hilkiah was the father of Azariah.
net.1CH_6_14: Azariah was the father of Seraiah, and Seraiah was the father of Jehozadak.
net.1CH_6_15: Jehozadak went into exile when the Lord sent the people of Judah and Jerusalem into exile by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
net.1CH_6_16: (6:1) The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
net.1CH_6_17: These are the names of the sons Gershom: Libni and Shimei.
net.1CH_6_18: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
net.1CH_6_19: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites by their families.
net.1CH_6_20: To Gershom: his son Libni, his son Jahath, his son Zimmah,
net.1CH_6_21: his son Joah, his son Iddo, his son Zerah, and his son Jeatherai.
net.1CH_6_22: The sons of Kohath: his son Amminadab, his son Korah, his son Assir,
net.1CH_6_23: his son Elkanah, his son Ebiasaph, his son Assir,
net.1CH_6_24: his son Tahath, his son Uriel, his son Uzziah, and his son Shaul.
net.1CH_6_25: The sons of Elkanah: Amasai, Ahimoth,
net.1CH_6_26: his son Elkanah, his son Zophai, his son Nahath,
net.1CH_6_27: his son Eliab, his son Jeroham, and his son Elkanah.
net.1CH_6_28: The sons of Samuel: Joel the firstborn and Abijah the second oldest.
net.1CH_6_29: The descendants of Merari: Mahli, his son Libni, his son Shimei, his son Uzzah,
net.1CH_6_30: his son Shimea, his son Haggiah, and his son Asaiah. Professional Musicians
net.1CH_6_31: These are the men David put in charge of music in the Lord’s sanctuary, after the ark was placed there.
net.1CH_6_32: They performed music before the sanctuary of the meeting tent until Solomon built the Lord’s temple in Jerusalem. They carried out their tasks according to regulations.
net.1CH_6_33: These are the ones who served along with their sons: From the Kohathites: Heman the musician, son of Joel, son of Samuel,
net.1CH_6_34: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Toah,
net.1CH_6_35: son of Zuph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasai,
net.1CH_6_36: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,
net.1CH_6_37: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah,
net.1CH_6_38: son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel.
net.1CH_6_39: Serving beside him was his fellow Levite Asaph, son of Berechiah, son of Shimea,
net.1CH_6_40: son of Michael, son of Baaseiah, son of Malkijah,
net.1CH_6_41: son of Ethni, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah,
net.1CH_6_42: son of Ethan, son of Zimmah, son of Shimei,
net.1CH_6_43: son of Jahath, son of Gershom, son of Levi.
net.1CH_6_44: Serving beside them were their fellow Levites, the descendants of Merari, led by Ethan, son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch,
net.1CH_6_45: son of Hashabiah, son of Amaziah, son of Hilkiah,
net.1CH_6_46: son of Amzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer,
net.1CH_6_47: son of Mahli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.
net.1CH_6_48: The rest of their fellow Levites were assigned to perform the remaining tasks at God’s sanctuary.
net.1CH_6_49: But Aaron and his descendants offered sacrifices on the altar for burnt offerings and on the altar for incense as they had been assigned to do in the Most Holy Sanctuary. They made atonement for Israel, just as God’s servant Moses had ordered.
net.1CH_6_50: These were the descendants of Aaron: His son Eleazar, his son Phinehas, his son Abishua,
net.1CH_6_51: his son Bukki, his son Uzzi, his son Zerahiah,
net.1CH_6_52: his son Meraioth, his son Amariah, his son Ahitub,
net.1CH_6_53: his son Zadok, and his son Ahimaaz.
net.1CH_6_54: These were the areas where Aaron’s descendants lived: The following belonged to the Kohathite clan, for they received the first allotment:
net.1CH_6_55: They were allotted Hebron in the territory of Judah, as well as its surrounding pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_56: (But the city’s land and nearby towns were allotted to Caleb son of Jephunneh.)
net.1CH_6_57: The descendants of Aaron were also allotted as cities of refuge Hebron, Libnah and its pasturelands, Jattir, Eshtemoa and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_58: Hilez and its pasturelands, Debir and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_59: Ashan and its pasturelands, and Beth Shemesh and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_60: Within the territory of the tribe of Benjamin they were allotted Geba and its pasturelands, Alemeth and its pasturelands, and Anathoth and its pasturelands. Their clans were allotted thirteen cities in all.
net.1CH_6_61: The rest of Kohath’s descendants were allotted ten cities in the territory of the half-tribe of Manasseh.
net.1CH_6_62: The clans of Gershom’s descendants received thirteen cities within the territory of the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh (in Bashan).
net.1CH_6_63: The clans of Merari’s descendants were allotted twelve cities within the territory of the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
net.1CH_6_64: So the Israelites gave to the Levites these cities and their pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_65: They allotted these previously named cities from the territory of the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin.
net.1CH_6_66: The clans of Kohath’s descendants also received cities as their territory within the tribe of Ephraim.
net.1CH_6_67: They were allotted as cities of refuge Shechem and its pasturelands (in the hill country of Ephraim), Gezer and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_68: Jokmeam and its pasturelands, Beth Horon and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_69: Aijalon and its pasturelands, and Gath Rimmon and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_70: Within the territory of the half-tribe of Manasseh, the rest of Kohath’s descendants received Aner and its pasturelands and Bileam and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_71: The following belonged to Gershom’s descendants: Within the territory of the half-tribe of Manasseh: Golan in Bashan and its pasturelands and Ashtaroth and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_72: Within the territory of the tribe of Issachar: Kedesh and its pasturelands, Daberath and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_73: Ramoth and its pasturelands, and Anem and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_74: Within the territory of the tribe of Asher: Mashal and its pasturelands, Abdon and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_75: Hukok and its pasturelands, and Rehob and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_76: Within the territory of the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee and its pasturelands, Hammon and its pasturelands, and Kiriathaim and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_77: The following belonged to the rest of Merari’s descendants: Within the territory of the tribe of Zebulun: Rimmono and its pasturelands, and Tabor and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_78: Within the territory of the tribe of Reuben across the Jordan River east of Jericho: Bezer in the wilderness and its pasturelands, Jahzah and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_79: Kedemoth and its pasturelands, and Mephaath and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_6_80: Within the territory of the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead and its pasturelands, Mahanaim and its pasturelands,
net.1CH_6_81: Heshbon and its pasturelands, and Jazer and its pasturelands.
net.1CH_7_1: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron—four in all.
net.1CH_7_2: The sons of Tola: Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Jibsam, and Samuel. They were leaders of their families. In the time of David there were 22,600 warriors listed in Tola’s genealogical records.
net.1CH_7_3: The son of Uzzi: Izrahiah. The sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah. All five were leaders.
net.1CH_7_4: According to the genealogical records of their families, they had 36,000 warriors available for battle, for they had numerous wives and sons.
net.1CH_7_5: Altogether the genealogical records of the clans of Issachar listed 87,000 warriors. Benjamin’s Descendants
net.1CH_7_6: The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Beker, and Jediael—three in all.
net.1CH_7_7: The sons of Bela: Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri. The five of them were leaders of their families. There were 22,034 warriors listed in their genealogical records.
net.1CH_7_8: The sons of Beker: Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these were the sons of Beker.
net.1CH_7_9: There were 20,200 family leaders and warriors listed in their genealogical records.
net.1CH_7_10: The son of Jediael: Bilhan. The sons of Bilhan: Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Kenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.
net.1CH_7_11: All these were the sons of Jediael. There were 17,200 family leaders and warriors who were capable of marching out to battle.
net.1CH_7_12: The Shuppites and Huppites were descendants of Ir; the Hushites were descendants of Aher. Naphtali’s Descendants
net.1CH_7_13: The sons of Naphtali: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum —sons of Bilhah. Manasseh’s Descendants
net.1CH_7_14: The sons of Manasseh: Asriel, who was born to Manasseh’s Aramean concubine. She also gave birth to Makir the father of Gilead.
net.1CH_7_15: Now Makir married a wife from the Huppites and Shuppites. (His sister’s name was Maacah.) Zelophehad was Manasseh’s second son; he had only daughters.
net.1CH_7_16: Maacah, Makir’s wife, gave birth to a son, whom she named Peresh. His brother was Sheresh, and his sons were Ulam and Rekem.
net.1CH_7_17: The son of Ulam: Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead, son of Makir, son of Manasseh.
net.1CH_7_18: His sister Hammoleketh gave birth to Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
net.1CH_7_19: The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam. Ephraim’s Descendants
net.1CH_7_20: The descendants of Ephraim: Shuthelah, his son Bered, his son Tahath, his son Eleadah, his son Tahath,
net.1CH_7_21: his son Zabad, his son Shuthelah (Ezer and Elead were killed by the men of Gath, who were natives of the land, when they went down to steal their cattle.
net.1CH_7_22: Their father Ephraim mourned for them many days and his brothers came to console him.
net.1CH_7_23: He slept with his wife; she became pregnant and gave birth to a son. Ephraim named him Beriah because tragedy had come to his family.
net.1CH_7_24: His daughter was Sheerah, who built Lower and Upper Beth Horon, as well as Uzzen Sheerah),
net.1CH_7_25: his son Rephah, his son Resheph, his son Telah, his son Tahan,
net.1CH_7_26: his son Ladan, his son Ammihud, his son Elishama,
net.1CH_7_27: his son Nun, and his son Joshua.
net.1CH_7_28: Their property and settlements included Bethel and its surrounding towns, Naaran to the east, Gezer and its surrounding towns to the west, and Shechem and its surrounding towns as far as Ayyah and its surrounding towns.
net.1CH_7_29: On the border of Manasseh’s territory were Beth Shean and its surrounding towns, Taanach and its surrounding towns, Megiddo and its surrounding towns, and Dor and its surrounding towns. The descendants of Joseph, Israel’s son, lived here. Asher’s Descendants
net.1CH_7_30: The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, and Beriah. Serah was their sister.
net.1CH_7_31: The sons of Beriah: Heber and Malkiel, who was the father of Birzaith.
net.1CH_7_32: Heber was the father of Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and Shua their sister.
net.1CH_7_33: The sons of Japhlet: Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These were Japhlet’s sons.
net.1CH_7_34: The sons of his brother Shemer: Rohgah, Hubbah, and Aram.
net.1CH_7_35: The sons of his brother Helem: Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
net.1CH_7_36: The sons of Zophah: Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
net.1CH_7_37: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
net.1CH_7_38: The sons of Jether: Jephunneh, Pispah, and Ara.
net.1CH_7_39: The sons of Ulla: Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
net.1CH_7_40: All these were the descendants of Asher. They were the leaders of their families, the most capable men, who were warriors and served as head chiefs. There were 26,000 warriors listed in their genealogical records as capable of doing battle.
net.1CH_8_1: Benjamin was the father of Bela, his firstborn; Ashbel was born second, Aharah third,
net.1CH_8_3: Bela’s sons were Addar, Gera, Abihud,
net.1CH_8_6: These were the descendants of Ehud who were leaders of the families living in Geba who were forced to move to Manahath:
net.1CH_8_7: Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera, who moved them. Gera was the father of Uzzah and Ahihud.
net.1CH_8_8: Shaharaim fathered sons in Moab after he divorced his wives Hushim and Baara.
net.1CH_8_9: By his wife Hodesh he fathered Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malkam,
net.1CH_8_10: Jeuz, Sakia, and Mirmah. These were his sons; they were family leaders.
net.1CH_8_11: By Hushim he fathered Abitub and Elpaal.
net.1CH_8_12: The sons of Elpaal: Eber, Misham, Shemed (who built Ono and Lod, as well as its surrounding towns),
net.1CH_8_13: Beriah, and Shema. They were leaders of the families living in Aijalon and chased out the inhabitants of Gath.
net.1CH_8_16: Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were the sons of Beriah.
net.1CH_8_18: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were the sons of Elpaal.
net.1CH_8_21: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were the sons of Shimei.
net.1CH_8_25: Iphdeiah, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.
net.1CH_8_27: Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zikri were the sons of Jeroham.
net.1CH_8_28: These were the family leaders listed in the genealogical records; they lived in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_8_29: The father of Gibeon lived in Gibeon; his wife’s name was Maacah.
net.1CH_8_30: His firstborn son was Abdon, followed by Zur, Kish, Baal, Nadab,
net.1CH_8_32: Mikloth was the father of Shimeah. They also lived near their relatives in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_8_33: Ner was the father of Kish, and Kish was the father of Saul. Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malki-Shua, Abinadab, and Eshbaal.
net.1CH_8_34: The son of Jonathan: Meribbaal. Meribbaal was the father of Micah.
net.1CH_8_35: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
net.1CH_8_36: Ahaz was the father of Jehoaddah, and Jehoaddah was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri was the father of Moza,
net.1CH_8_37: and Moza was the father of Binea. His son was Raphah, whose son was Eleasah, whose son was Azel.
net.1CH_8_38: Azel had six sons: Azrikam his firstborn, followed by Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.
net.1CH_8_39: The sons of his brother Eshek: Ulam was his firstborn, Jeush second, and Eliphelet third.
net.1CH_8_40: The sons of Ulam were warriors who were adept archers. They had many sons and grandsons, a total of 150. All these were the descendants of Benjamin.
net.1CH_9_1: Genealogical records were kept for all Israel; they are recorded in the Scroll of the Kings of Israel. Exiles Who Resettled in Jerusalem The people of Judah were carried away to Babylon because of their unfaithfulness.
net.1CH_9_2: The first to resettle on their property and in their cities were some Israelites, priests, Levites, and temple servants.
net.1CH_9_3: Some from the tribes of Judah, Benjamin, and Ephraim and Manasseh settled in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_9_4: The settlers included: Uthai son of Ammihud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, who was a descendant of Perez son of Judah.
net.1CH_9_5: From the Shilonites: Asaiah the firstborn and his sons.
net.1CH_9_6: From the descendants of Zerah: Jeuel. Their relatives numbered 690.
net.1CH_9_7: From the descendants of Benjamin: Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Hodaviah, son of Hassenuah;
net.1CH_9_8: Ibneiah son of Jeroham; Elah son of Uzzi, son of Mikri; and Meshullam son of Shephatiah, son of Reuel, son of Ibnijah.
net.1CH_9_9: Their relatives, listed in their genealogical records, numbered 956. All these men were leaders of their families.
net.1CH_9_10: From the priests: Jedaiah; Jehoiarib; Jakin;
net.1CH_9_11: Azariah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub the leader in God’s temple;
net.1CH_9_12: Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malkijah; and Maasai son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer.
net.1CH_9_13: Their relatives, who were leaders of their families, numbered 1,760. They were capable men who were assigned to carry out the various tasks of service in God’s temple.
net.1CH_9_14: From the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah a descendant of Merari;
net.1CH_9_15: Bakbakkar; Heresh; Galal; Mattaniah son of Mika, son of Zikri, son of Asaph;
net.1CH_9_16: Obadiah son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun; and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who lived among the settlements of the Netophathites.
net.1CH_9_17: The gatekeepers were: Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their brothers. Shallum was the leader;
net.1CH_9_18: he serves to this day at the King’s Gate on the east. These were the gatekeepers from the camp of the descendants of Levi.
net.1CH_9_19: Shallum son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah, and his relatives from his family (the Korahites) were assigned to guard the entrance to the sanctuary. Their ancestors had guarded the entrance to the Lord’s dwelling place.
net.1CH_9_20: Phinehas son of Eleazar had been their leader in earlier times, and the Lord was with him.
net.1CH_9_21: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was the guard at the entrance to the meeting tent.
net.1CH_9_22: All those selected to be gatekeepers at the entrances numbered 212. Their names were recorded in the genealogical records of their settlements. David and Samuel the prophet had appointed them to their positions.
net.1CH_9_23: They and their descendants were assigned to guard the gates of the Lord’s sanctuary (that is, the tabernacle).
net.1CH_9_24: The gatekeepers were posted on all four sides—east, west, north, and south.
net.1CH_9_25: Their relatives, who lived in their settlements, came from time to time and served with them for seven-day periods.
net.1CH_9_26: The four head gatekeepers, who were Levites, were assigned to guard the storerooms and treasuries in God’s sanctuary.
net.1CH_9_27: They would spend the night in their posts all around God’s sanctuary, for they were assigned to guard it and would open it with the key every morning.
net.1CH_9_28: Some of them were in charge of the articles used by those who served; they counted them when they brought them in and when they brought them out.
net.1CH_9_29: Some of them were in charge of the equipment and articles of the sanctuary, as well as the flour, wine, olive oil, incense, and spices.
net.1CH_9_30: (But some of the priests mixed the spices.)
net.1CH_9_31: Mattithiah, a Levite, the firstborn son of Shallum the Korahite, was in charge of baking the bread for offerings.
net.1CH_9_32: Some of the Kohathites, their relatives, were in charge of preparing the bread that is displayed each Sabbath.
net.1CH_9_33: The musicians and Levite family leaders stayed in rooms at the sanctuary and were exempt from other duties, for day and night they had to carry out their assigned tasks.
net.1CH_9_34: These were the family leaders of the Levites, as listed in their genealogical records. They lived in Jerusalem. Jeiel’s Descendants
net.1CH_9_35: Jeiel (the father of Gibeon) lived in Gibeon. His wife was Maacah.
net.1CH_9_36: His firstborn son was Abdon, followed by Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
net.1CH_9_38: Mikloth was the father of Shimeam. They also lived near their relatives in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_9_39: Ner was the father of Kish, and Kish was the father of Saul. Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malki-Shua, Abinadab, and Eshbaal.
net.1CH_9_40: The son of Jonathan: Meribbaal, who was the father of Micah.
net.1CH_9_41: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz.
net.1CH_9_42: Ahaz was the father of Jarah, and Jarah was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri was the father of Moza,
net.1CH_9_43: and Moza was the father of Binea. His son was Rephaiah, whose son was Eleasah, whose son was Azel.
net.1CH_9_44: Azel had six sons: Azrikam his firstborn, followed by Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. These were the sons of Azel.
net.1CH_10_1: Now the Philistines fought against Israel. The Israelites fled before the Philistines and many of them fell dead on Mount Gilboa.
net.1CH_10_2: The Philistines stayed right on the heels of Saul and his sons. They struck down Saul’s sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malki-Shua.
net.1CH_10_3: The battle was thick around Saul; the archers spotted him and wounded him.
net.1CH_10_4: Saul told his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword and stab me with it. Otherwise these uncircumcised people will come and torture me.” But his armor-bearer refused to do it, because he was very afraid. So Saul took the sword and fell on it.
net.1CH_10_5: When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his sword and died.
net.1CH_10_6: So Saul and his three sons died; his whole household died together.
net.1CH_10_7: When all the Israelites who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their cities and fled. The Philistines came and occupied them.
net.1CH_10_8: The next day, when the Philistines came to strip loot from the corpses, they discovered Saul and his sons lying dead on Mount Gilboa.
net.1CH_10_9: They stripped his corpse, and then carried off his head and his armor. They sent messengers throughout the land of the Philistines proclaiming the news to their idols and their people.
net.1CH_10_10: They placed his armor in the temple of their gods and hung his head in the temple of Dagon.
net.1CH_10_11: When all the residents of Jabesh Gilead heard about everything the Philistines had done to Saul,
net.1CH_10_12: all the warriors went and recovered the bodies of Saul and his sons and brought them to Jabesh. They buried their remains under the oak tree in Jabesh and fasted for seven days.
net.1CH_10_13: So Saul died because he was unfaithful to the Lord and did not obey the Lord’s instructions; he even tried to conjure up underworld spirits.
net.1CH_10_14: He did not seek the Lord’s guidance, so the Lord killed him and transferred the kingdom to David son of Jesse.
net.1CH_11_1: All Israel joined David at Hebron and said, “Look, we are your very flesh and blood!
net.1CH_11_2: In the past, even when Saul was king, you were Israel’s commanding general. The Lord your God said to you, ‘You will shepherd my people Israel; you will rule over my people Israel.’”
net.1CH_11_3: When all the leaders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, David made a covenant with them in Hebron before the Lord. They anointed David king over Israel, in keeping with the Lord’s message that came through Samuel. David Conquers Jerusalem
net.1CH_11_4: David and the whole Israelite army advanced to Jerusalem (that is, Jebus). (The Jebusites, the land’s original inhabitants, lived there.)
net.1CH_11_5: The residents of Jebus said to David, “You cannot invade this place!” But David captured the fortress of Zion (that is, the City of David).
net.1CH_11_6: David said, “Whoever attacks the Jebusites first will become commanding general!” So Joab son of Zeruiah attacked first and became commander.
net.1CH_11_7: David lived in the fortress; for this reason it is called the City of David.
net.1CH_11_8: He built up the city around it, from the terrace to the surrounding walls; Joab restored the rest of the city.
net.1CH_11_9: David’s power steadily grew, for the Lord of Heaven’s Armies was with him. David’s Warriors
net.1CH_11_10: These were the leaders of David’s warriors who, together with all Israel, stood courageously with him in his kingdom by installing him as king, in keeping with the Lord’s message concerning Israel.
net.1CH_11_11: This is the list of David’s warriors: Jashobeam, a Hacmonite, was head of the officers. He killed 300 men with his spear in a single battle.
net.1CH_11_12: Next in command was Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite. He was one of the three elite warriors.
net.1CH_11_13: He was with David in Pas Dammim when the Philistines assembled there for battle. In an area of the field that was full of barley, the army retreated before the Philistines,
net.1CH_11_14: but then they made a stand in the middle of that area. They defended it and defeated the Philistines; the Lord gave them a great victory.
net.1CH_11_15: Three of the thirty leaders went down to David at the rocky cliff at the cave of Adullam, while a Philistine force was camped in the Valley of Rephaim.
net.1CH_11_16: David was in the stronghold at the time, while a Philistine garrison was in Bethlehem.
net.1CH_11_17: David was thirsty and said, “How I wish someone would give me some water to drink from the cistern in Bethlehem near the city gate!”
net.1CH_11_18: So the three elite warriors broke through the Philistine forces and drew some water from the cistern in Bethlehem near the city gate. They carried it back to David, but David refused to drink it. He poured it out as a drink offering to the Lord
net.1CH_11_19: and said, “God forbid that I should do this! Should I drink the blood of these men who risked their lives?” Because they risked their lives to bring it to him, he refused to drink it. Such were the exploits of the three elite warriors.
net.1CH_11_20: Abishai the brother of Joab was head of the three elite warriors. He killed 300 men with his spear and gained fame along with the three elite warriors.
net.1CH_11_21: From the three he was given double honor and he became their officer, even though he was not one of them.
net.1CH_11_22: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a brave warrior from Kabzeel who performed great exploits. He struck down the two sons of Ariel of Moab; he also went down and killed a lion inside a cistern on a snowy day.
net.1CH_11_23: He even killed an Egyptian who was 7½ feet tall. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand as big as the crossbeam of a weaver’s loom; Benaiah attacked him with a club. He grabbed the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand and killed him with his own spear.
net.1CH_11_24: Such were the exploits of Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who gained fame along with the three elite warriors.
net.1CH_11_25: He received honor from the thirty warriors, though he was not one of the three elite warriors. David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
net.1CH_11_26: The mighty warriors were: Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo, from Bethlehem,
net.1CH_11_27: Shammoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite,
net.1CH_11_28: Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Abiezer the Anathothite,
net.1CH_11_29: Sibbekai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
net.1CH_11_30: Maharai the Netophathite, Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite,
net.1CH_11_31: Ithai son of Ribai from Gibeah in Benjaminite territory, Benaiah the Pirathonite,
net.1CH_11_32: Hurai from the valleys of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
net.1CH_11_33: Azmaveth the Baharumite, Eliahba the Shaalbonite,
net.1CH_11_34: the sons of Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan son of Shageh the Hararite,
net.1CH_11_35: Ahiam son of Sakar the Hararite, Eliphal son of Ur,
net.1CH_11_36: Hepher the Mekerathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
net.1CH_11_37: Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai son of Ezbai,
net.1CH_11_38: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri,
net.1CH_11_39: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Beerothite, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,
net.1CH_11_40: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
net.1CH_11_41: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Achli,
net.1CH_11_42: Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite, leader of the Reubenites and the thirty warriors with him,
net.1CH_11_43: Hanan son of Maacah, Joshaphat the Mithnite,
net.1CH_11_44: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel, the sons of Hotham the Aroerite,
net.1CH_11_45: Jediael son of Shimri, and Joha his brother, the Tizite,
net.1CH_11_46: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah, the sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite,
net.1CH_11_47: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.
net.1CH_12_1: These were the men who joined David in Ziklag, when he was banished from the presence of Saul son of Kish. (They were among the warriors who assisted him in battle.
net.1CH_12_2: They were armed with bows and could shoot arrows or sling stones right or left-handed. They were fellow tribesmen of Saul from Benjamin.) These were:
net.1CH_12_3: Ahiezer, the leader, and Joash, the sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite; Jeziel and Pelet, the sons of Azmaveth; Berachah, Jehu the Anathothite,
net.1CH_12_4: Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, one of the thirty warriors and their leader, (12:5) Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad the Gederathite,
net.1CH_12_5: (12:6) Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite,
net.1CH_12_6: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, who were Korahites,
net.1CH_12_7: and Joelah and Zebadiah, the sons of Jeroham from Gedor.
net.1CH_12_8: Some of the Gadites joined David at the stronghold in the wilderness. They were warriors who were trained for battle; they carried shields and spears. They were as fierce as lions and could run as quickly as gazelles across the hills.
net.1CH_12_9: Ezer was the leader, Obadiah the second in command, Eliab the third,
net.1CH_12_10: Mishmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth,
net.1CH_12_11: Attai the sixth, Eliel the seventh,
net.1CH_12_12: Johanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth,
net.1CH_12_13: Jeremiah the tenth, and Machbannai the eleventh.
net.1CH_12_14: These Gadites were military leaders; the least led a hundred men, the greatest a thousand.
net.1CH_12_15: They crossed the Jordan River in the first month, when it was overflowing its banks, and routed those living in all the valleys to the east and west.
net.1CH_12_16: Some from Benjamin and Judah also came to David’s stronghold.
net.1CH_12_17: David went out to meet them and said, “If you come to me in peace and want to help me, then I will make an alliance with you. But if you come to betray me to my enemies when I have not harmed you, may the God of our ancestors take notice and judge!”
net.1CH_12_18: But a spirit empowered Amasai, the leader of the group of warriors known as the Thirty, and he said: “We are yours, O David! We support you, O son of Jesse! May you greatly prosper. May those who help you prosper. Indeed your God helps you!” So David accepted them and made them leaders of raiding bands.
net.1CH_12_19: Some men from Manasseh joined David when he went with the Philistines to fight against Saul. (But in the end they did not help the Philistines because, after taking counsel, the Philistine lords sent David away, saying, “It would be disastrous for us if he deserts to his master Saul.”)
net.1CH_12_20: When David went to Ziklag, the men of Manasseh who joined him were Adnach, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, leaders of 1,000 soldiers each in the tribe of Manasseh.
net.1CH_12_21: They helped David fight against raiding bands, for all of them were warriors and leaders in the army.
net.1CH_12_22: Each day men came to help David until his army became very large. Support for David in Hebron
net.1CH_12_23: The following is a record of the armed warriors who came with their leaders and joined David in Hebron in order to make David king in Saul’s place, in accordance with the Lord’s decree:
net.1CH_12_24: From Judah came 6,800 trained warriors carrying shields and spears.
net.1CH_12_27: Jehoiada, the leader of Aaron’s descendants, brought 3,700 men with him,
net.1CH_12_28: along with Zadok, a young warrior, and 22 leaders from his family.
net.1CH_12_29: From Benjamin, Saul’s tribe, there were 3,000, most of whom, up to that time, had been loyal to Saul.
net.1CH_12_30: From Ephraim there were 20,800 warriors, who had brought fame to their families.
net.1CH_12_31: From the half-tribe of Manasseh there were 18,000 who had been designated by name to come and make David king.
net.1CH_12_32: From Issachar there were 200 leaders and all their relatives at their command—they understood the times and knew what Israel should do.
net.1CH_12_33: From Zebulun there were 50,000 warriors who were prepared for battle, equipped with all kinds of weapons, and ready to give their undivided loyalty.
net.1CH_12_34: From Naphtali there were 1,000 officers, along with 37,000 men carrying shields and spears.
net.1CH_12_35: From Dan there were 28,600 men prepared for battle.
net.1CH_12_36: From Asher there were 40,000 warriors prepared for battle.
net.1CH_12_37: From the other side of the Jordan, from Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, there were 120,000 men armed with all kinds of weapons.
net.1CH_12_38: All these men were warriors who were ready to march. They came to Hebron to make David king over all Israel by acclamation; all the rest of the Israelites also were in agreement that David should become king.
net.1CH_12_39: They spent three days feasting there with David, for their relatives had given them provisions.
net.1CH_12_40: Also their neighbors, from as far away as Issachar, Zebulun, and Naphtali, were bringing food on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen. There were large supplies of flour, fig cakes, raisins, wine, olive oil, beef, and lamb, for Israel was celebrating.
net.1CH_13_1: David consulted with his military officers, including those who led groups of a thousand and those who led groups of a hundred.
net.1CH_13_2: David said to the whole Israelite assembly, “If you so desire and the Lord our God approves, let’s spread the word to our brothers who remain in all the regions of Israel, and to the priests and Levites in their cities, so they may join us.
net.1CH_13_3: Let’s move the ark of our God back here, for we did not seek his will throughout Saul’s reign.”
net.1CH_13_4: The whole assembly agreed to do this, for the proposal seemed right to all the people.
net.1CH_13_5: So David assembled all Israel from the Shihor River in Egypt to Lebo Hamath, to bring the ark of God from Kiriath Jearim.
net.1CH_13_6: David and all Israel went up to Baalah (that is, Kiriath Jearim) in Judah to bring up from there the ark of God the Lord, who sits enthroned between the cherubim—the ark that is called by his Name.
net.1CH_13_7: They transported the ark of God on a new cart from the house of Abinadab; Uzzah and Ahio were guiding the cart,
net.1CH_13_8: while David and all Israel were energetically celebrating before God, singing and playing various stringed instruments, tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.
net.1CH_13_9: When they arrived at the threshing floor of Kidon, Uzzah reached out his hand to take hold of the ark, because the oxen stumbled.
net.1CH_13_10: The Lord was so furious with Uzzah, he killed him, because he reached out his hand and touched the ark. He died right there before God.
net.1CH_13_11: David was angry because the Lord attacked Uzzah; so he called that place Perez Uzzah, which remains its name to this very day.
net.1CH_13_12: David was afraid of God that day and said, “How will I ever be able to bring the ark of God up here?”
net.1CH_13_13: So David did not move the ark to the City of David; he left it in the house of Obed-Edom the Gittite.
net.1CH_13_14: The ark of God remained in Obed-Edom’s house for three months; the Lord blessed Obed-Edom’s family and everything that belonged to him.
net.1CH_14_1: King Hiram of Tyre sent messengers to David, along with cedar logs, stonemasons, and carpenters to build a palace for him.
net.1CH_14_2: David realized that the Lord had established him as king over Israel and that he had elevated his kingdom for the sake of his people Israel.
net.1CH_14_3: In Jerusalem David married more wives and fathered more sons and daughters.
net.1CH_14_4: These are the names of children born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
