net.1CH_14_8: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king of all Israel, all the Philistines marched up to confront him. When David heard about it, he marched out against them.
net.1CH_14_9: Now the Philistines had come and raided the Valley of Rephaim.
net.1CH_14_10: David asked God, “Should I march up against the Philistines? Will you hand them over to me?” The Lord said to him, “March up! I will hand them over to you!”
net.1CH_14_11: So they marched against Baal Perazim and David defeated them there. David said, “Using me as his instrument, God has burst out against my enemies like water bursts out.” So that place is called Baal Perazim.
net.1CH_14_12: The Philistines left their idols there, so David ordered that they be burned.
net.1CH_14_13: The Philistines again raided the valley.
net.1CH_14_14: So David again asked God what he should do. This time God told him, “Don’t march up after them; circle around them and come against them in front of the trees.
net.1CH_14_15: When you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the trees, then attack. For at that moment God is going before you to strike down the army of the Philistines.”
net.1CH_14_16: David did just as God commanded him, and they struck down the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
net.1CH_14_17: So David became famous in all the lands; the Lord caused all the nations to fear him.
net.1CH_15_1: David constructed buildings in the City of David; he then prepared a place for the ark of God and pitched a tent for it.
net.1CH_15_2: Then David said, “Only the Levites may carry the ark of God, for the Lord chose them to carry the ark of the Lord and to serve before him perpetually.”
net.1CH_15_3: David assembled all Israel at Jerusalem to bring the ark of the Lord up to the place he had prepared for it.
net.1CH_15_4: David gathered together the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:
net.1CH_15_5: From the descendants of Kohath: Uriel the leader and 120 of his relatives.
net.1CH_15_6: From the descendants of Merari: Asaiah the leader and 220 of his relatives.
net.1CH_15_7: From the descendants of Gershom: Joel the leader and 130 of his relatives.
net.1CH_15_8: From the descendants of Elizaphan: Shemaiah the leader and 200 of his relatives.
net.1CH_15_9: From the descendants of Hebron: Eliel the leader and 80 of his relatives.
net.1CH_15_10: From the descendants of Uzziel: Amminadab the leader and 112 of his relatives.
net.1CH_15_11: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar, along with the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.
net.1CH_15_12: He told them: “You are the leaders of the Levites’ families. You and your relatives must consecrate yourselves and bring the ark of the Lord God of Israel up to the place I have prepared for it.
net.1CH_15_13: The first time you did not carry it; that is why the Lord God attacked us, because we did not ask him about the proper way to carry it.”
net.1CH_15_14: The priests and Levites consecrated themselves so they could bring up the ark of the Lord God of Israel.
net.1CH_15_15: The descendants of Levi carried the ark of God on their shoulders with poles, just as Moses had commanded in keeping with the Lord’s instruction.
net.1CH_15_16: David told the leaders of the Levites to appoint some of their relatives as musicians; they were to play various instruments, including stringed instruments and cymbals, and to sing loudly and joyfully.
net.1CH_15_17: So the Levites appointed Heman son of Joel; one of his relatives, Asaph son of Berechiah; one of the descendants of Merari, Ethan son of Kushaiah;
net.1CH_15_18: along with some of their relatives who were second in rank, including Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-Edom, and Jeiel, the gatekeepers.
net.1CH_15_19: The musicians Heman, Asaph, and Ethan were to sound the bronze cymbals;
net.1CH_15_20: Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah were to play the harps according to the alamoth style;
net.1CH_15_21: Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-Edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah were to play the lyres according to the sheminith style, as led by the director;
net.1CH_15_22: Kenaniah, the leader of the Levites, was in charge of transport, for he was well-informed on this matter;
net.1CH_15_23: Berechiah and Elkanah were guardians of the ark;
net.1CH_15_24: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer the priests were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God; Obed-Edom and Jehiel were also guardians of the ark.
net.1CH_15_25: So David, the leaders of Israel, and the commanders of units of a thousand went to bring up the ark of the Lord’s covenant from the house of Obed-Edom with celebration.
net.1CH_15_26: When God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the Lord’s covenant, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
net.1CH_15_27: David was wrapped in a linen robe, as were all the Levites carrying the ark, the musicians, and Kenaniah the supervisor of transport and the musicians; David also wore a linen ephod.
net.1CH_15_28: All Israel brought up the ark of the Lord’s covenant; they were shouting, blowing trumpets, sounding cymbals, and playing stringed instruments.
net.1CH_15_29: As the ark of the Lord’s covenant entered the City of David, Michal, Saul’s daughter, looked out the window. When she saw King David jumping and celebrating, she despised him.
net.1CH_16_1: They brought the ark of God and put it in the middle of the tent David had pitched for it. Then they offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings before God.
net.1CH_16_2: When David finished offering burnt sacrifices and peace offerings, he pronounced a blessing over the people in the Lord’s name.
net.1CH_16_3: He then handed out to each Israelite man and woman a loaf of bread, a date cake, and a raisin cake.
net.1CH_16_4: He appointed some of the Levites to serve before the ark of the Lord, to offer prayers, songs of thanks, and hymns to the Lord God of Israel.
net.1CH_16_5: Asaph was the leader and Zechariah second-in-command, followed by Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-Edom, and Jeiel. They were to play stringed instruments, Asaph was to sound the cymbals,
net.1CH_16_6: and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel were to blow trumpets regularly before the ark of God’s covenant. David Thanks God
net.1CH_16_7: That day David first gave to Asaph and his colleagues this song of thanks to the Lord.
net.1CH_16_8: Give thanks to the Lord! Call on his name! Make known his accomplishments among the nations.
net.1CH_16_9: Sing to him! Make music to him! Tell about all his miraculous deeds.
net.1CH_16_10: Boast about his holy name. Let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
net.1CH_16_11: Seek the Lord and the strength he gives. Seek his presence continually!
net.1CH_16_12: Recall the miraculous deeds he performed, his mighty acts and the judgments he decreed,
net.1CH_16_13: O children of Israel, God’s servant, you descendants of Jacob, God’s chosen ones!
net.1CH_16_14: He is the Lord our God; he carries out judgment throughout the earth.
net.1CH_16_15: Remember continually his covenantal decree, the promise he made to a thousand generations—
net.1CH_16_16: the promise he made to Abraham, the promise he made by oath to Isaac!
net.1CH_16_17: He gave it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as a lasting promise,
net.1CH_16_18: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as the portion of your inheritance.”
net.1CH_16_19: When they were few in number, just a very few, and foreign residents within it,
net.1CH_16_20: they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another.
net.1CH_16_21: He let no one oppress them; he disciplined kings for their sake,
net.1CH_16_22: saying, “Don’t touch my anointed ones! Don’t harm my prophets!”
net.1CH_16_23: Sing to the Lord, all the earth! Announce every day how he delivers.
net.1CH_16_24: Tell the nations about his splendor, tell all the nations about his miraculous deeds.
net.1CH_16_25: For the Lord is great and certainly worthy of praise, he is more awesome than all gods.
net.1CH_16_26: For all the gods of the nations are worthless, but the Lord made the heavens.
net.1CH_16_27: Majestic splendor emanates from him, he is the source of strength and joy.
net.1CH_16_28: Ascribe to the Lord, O families of the nations, ascribe to the Lord splendor and strength!
net.1CH_16_29: Ascribe to the Lord the splendor he deserves! Bring an offering and enter his presence! Worship the Lord in holy attire!
net.1CH_16_30: Tremble before him, all the earth! The world is established, it cannot be moved.
net.1CH_16_31: Let the heavens rejoice, and the earth be happy! Let the nations say, “The Lord reigns!”
net.1CH_16_32: Let the sea and everything in it shout! Let the fields and everything in them celebrate!
net.1CH_16_33: Then let the trees of the forest shout with joy before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth!
net.1CH_16_34: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good and his loyal love endures.
net.1CH_16_35: Say this prayer: “Deliver us, O God who delivers us! Gather us! Rescue us from the nations! Then we will give thanks to your holy name, and boast about your praiseworthy deeds.”
net.1CH_16_36: May the Lord God of Israel be praised, in the future and forevermore. Then all the people said, “We agree! Praise the Lord.” David Appoints Worship Leaders
net.1CH_16_37: David left Asaph and his colleagues there before the ark of the Lord’s covenant to serve before the ark regularly and fulfill each day’s requirements,
net.1CH_16_38: including Obed-Edom and sixty-eight colleagues. Obed-Edom son of Jeduthun and Hosah were gatekeepers.
net.1CH_16_39: Zadok the priest and his fellow priests served before the Lord’s tabernacle at the worship center in Gibeon,
net.1CH_16_40: regularly offering burnt sacrifices to the Lord on the altar for burnt sacrifice, morning and evening, according to what is prescribed in the law of the Lord which he charged Israel to observe.
net.1CH_16_41: Joining them were Heman, Jeduthun, and the rest of those chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the Lord. (For his loyal love endures!)
net.1CH_16_42: Heman and Jeduthun were in charge of the music, including the trumpets, cymbals, and the other musical instruments used in praising God. The sons of Jeduthun guarded the entrance.
net.1CH_16_43: Then all the people returned to their homes, and David went to pronounce a blessing on his family.
net.1CH_17_1: When David had settled into his palace, he said to Nathan the prophet, “Look, I am living in a palace made from cedar, while the ark of the Lord’s covenant is under a tent.”
net.1CH_17_2: Nathan said to David, “You should do whatever you have in mind, for God is with you.”
net.1CH_17_4: “Go, tell my servant David: ‘This is what the Lord says: “You must not build me a house in which to live.
net.1CH_17_5: For I have not lived in a house from the time I brought Israel up from Egypt to the present day. I have lived in a tent that has been in various places.
net.1CH_17_6: Wherever I moved throughout Israel, I did not say to any of the leaders whom I appointed to care for my people Israel, ‘Why have you not built me a house made from cedar?’”’
net.1CH_17_7: “So now, say this to my servant David: ‘This is what the Lord of Heaven’s Armies says: “I took you from the pasture and from your work as a shepherd to make you a leader of my people Israel.
net.1CH_17_8: I was with you wherever you went and I defeated all your enemies before you. Now I will make you as famous as the great men of the earth.
net.1CH_17_9: I will establish a place for my people Israel and settle them there; they will live there and not be disturbed anymore. Violent men will not oppress them again, as they did in the beginning
net.1CH_17_10: and during the time when I appointed judges to lead my people Israel. I will subdue all your enemies. “‘“I declare to you that the Lord will build a dynastic house for you!
net.1CH_17_11: When the time comes for you to die, I will raise up your descendant, one of your own sons, to succeed you, and I will establish his kingdom.
net.1CH_17_12: He will build me a house, and I will make his dynasty permanent.
net.1CH_17_13: I will become his father and he will become my son. I will never withhold my loyal love from him, as I withheld it from the one who ruled before you.
net.1CH_17_14: I will put him in permanent charge of my house and my kingdom; his dynasty will be permanent.”’”
net.1CH_17_15: Nathan told David all these words that were revealed to him. David Praises God
net.1CH_17_16: King David went in, sat before the Lord, and said: “Who am I, O Lord God, and what is my family, that you should have brought me to this point?
net.1CH_17_17: And you did not stop there, O God! You have also spoken about the future of your servant’s family. You have revealed to me what men long to know, O Lord God.
net.1CH_17_18: What more can David say to you? You have honored your servant; you have given your servant special recognition.
net.1CH_17_19: O Lord, for the sake of your servant and according to your will, you have done this great thing in order to reveal your greatness.
net.1CH_17_20: O Lord, there is none like you; there is no God besides you! What we heard is true!
net.1CH_17_21: And who is like your people, Israel, a unique nation in the earth? Their God went to claim a nation for himself! You made a name for yourself by doing great and awesome deeds when you drove out nations before your people whom you had delivered from the Egyptian empire and its gods.
net.1CH_17_22: You made Israel your very own nation for all time. You, O Lord, became their God.
net.1CH_17_23: So now, O Lord, may the promise you made about your servant and his family become a permanent reality! Do as you promised,
net.1CH_17_24: so it may become a reality and you may gain lasting fame, as people say, ‘The Lord of Heaven’s Armies is the God of Israel.’ The dynasty of your servant David will be established before you,
net.1CH_17_25: for you, my God, have revealed to your servant that you will build a dynasty for him. That is why your servant has had the courage to pray to you.
net.1CH_17_26: Now, O Lord, you are the true God; you have made this good promise to your servant.
net.1CH_17_27: Now you are willing to bless your servant’s dynasty so that it may stand permanently before you, for you, O Lord, have blessed it and it will be blessed from now on into the future.”
net.1CH_18_1: Later David defeated the Philistines and subdued them. He took Gath and its surrounding towns away from the Philistines.
net.1CH_18_2: He defeated the Moabites; the Moabites became David’s subjects and brought tribute.
net.1CH_18_3: David defeated King Hadadezer of Zobah as far as Hamath, when he went to extend his authority to the Euphrates River.
net.1CH_18_4: David seized from him 1,000 chariots, 7,000 charioteers, and 20,000 infantrymen . David cut the hamstrings of all but 100 of Hadadezer’s chariot horses.
net.1CH_18_5: The Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, but David killed 22,000 of the Arameans.
net.1CH_18_6: David placed garrisons in the territory of the Arameans of Damascus; the Arameans became David’s subjects and brought tribute. The Lord protected David wherever he campaigned.
net.1CH_18_7: David took the golden shields which Hadadezer’s servants had carried and brought them to Jerusalem.
net.1CH_18_8: From Tibhath and Kun, Hadadezer’s cities, David took a great deal of bronze. (Solomon used it to make the big bronze basin called “The Sea,” the pillars, and other bronze items.)
net.1CH_18_9: When King Tou of Hamath heard that David had defeated the entire army of King Hadadezer of Zobah,
net.1CH_18_10: he sent his son Hadoram to King David to extend his best wishes and to pronounce a blessing on him for his victory over Hadadezer, for Tou had been at war with Hadadezer. He also sent various items made of gold, silver, and bronze.
net.1CH_18_11: King David dedicated these things to the Lord, along with the silver and gold which he had carried off from all the nations, including Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.
net.1CH_18_12: Abishai son of Zeruiah killed 18,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
net.1CH_18_13: He placed garrisons in Edom, and all the Edomites became David’s subjects. The Lord protected David wherever he campaigned. David’s Officials
net.1CH_18_14: David reigned over all Israel; he guaranteed justice for all his people.
net.1CH_18_15: Joab son of Zeruiah was commanding general of the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was secretary;
net.1CH_18_16: Zadok son of Ahitub and Abimelech son of Abiathar were priests; Shavsha was scribe;
net.1CH_18_17: Benaiah son of Jehoiada supervised the Kerethites and Pelethites; and David’s sons were the king’s leading officials.
net.1CH_19_1: Later King Nahash of the Ammonites died and his son succeeded him.
net.1CH_19_2: David said, “I will express my loyalty to Hanun son of Nahash, for his father was loyal to me.” So David sent messengers to express his sympathy over his father’s death. When David’s servants entered Ammonite territory to visit Hanun and express the king’s sympathy,
net.1CH_19_3: the Ammonite officials said to Hanun, “Do you really think David is trying to honor your father by sending these messengers to express his sympathy? No, his servants have come to you so they can get information and spy out the land!”
net.1CH_19_4: So Hanun seized David’s servants and shaved their beards off. He cut off the lower part of their robes so that their buttocks were exposed and then sent them away.
net.1CH_19_5: People came and told David what had happened to the men, so he sent messengers to meet them, for the men were thoroughly humiliated. The king said, “Stay in Jericho until your beards grow again; then you may come back.”
net.1CH_19_6: When the Ammonites realized that David was disgusted with them, Hanun and the Ammonites sent 1,000 talents of silver to hire chariots and charioteers from Aram Naharaim, Aram Maacah, and Zobah.
net.1CH_19_7: They hired 32,000 chariots, along with the king of Maacah and his army, who came and camped in front of Medeba. The Ammonites also assembled from their cities and marched out to do battle.
net.1CH_19_8: When David heard the news, he sent Joab and the entire army to meet them.
net.1CH_19_9: The Ammonites marched out and were deployed for battle at the entrance to the city, while the kings who had come were by themselves in the field.
net.1CH_19_10: When Joab saw that the battle would be fought on two fronts, he chose some of Israel’s best men and deployed them against the Arameans.
net.1CH_19_11: He put his brother Abishai in charge of the rest of the army and they were deployed against the Ammonites.
net.1CH_19_12: Joab said, “If the Arameans start to overpower me, you come to my rescue. If the Ammonites start to overpower you, I will come to your rescue.
net.1CH_19_13: Be strong! Let’s fight bravely for the sake of our people and the cities of our God! The Lord will do what he decides is best!”
net.1CH_19_14: So Joab and his men marched toward the Arameans to do battle, and they fled before him.
net.1CH_19_15: When the Ammonites saw the Arameans flee, they fled before Joab’s brother Abishai and withdrew into the city. Joab went back to Jerusalem.
net.1CH_19_16: When the Arameans realized they had been defeated by Israel, they sent for reinforcements from beyond the Euphrates River, led by Shophach the commanding general of Hadadezer’s army.
net.1CH_19_17: When David was informed, he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan River, and marched against them. David deployed his army against the Arameans for battle and they fought against him.
net.1CH_19_18: The Arameans fled before Israel. David killed 7,000 Aramean charioteers and 40,000 infantrymen; he also killed Shophach the commanding general.
net.1CH_19_19: When Hadadezer’s subjects saw they were defeated by Israel, they made peace with David and became his subjects. The Arameans were no longer willing to help the Ammonites.
net.1CH_20_1: In the spring, at the time when kings normally conduct wars, Joab led the army into battle and devastated the land of the Ammonites. He went and besieged Rabbah, while David stayed in Jerusalem. Joab defeated Rabbah and tore it down.
net.1CH_20_2: David took the crown from the head of their king and wore it (its weight was a talent of gold and it was set with precious stones). He took a large amount of plunder from the city.
net.1CH_20_3: He removed the city’s residents and made them labor with saws, iron picks, and axes. This was his policy with all the Ammonite cities. Then David and all the army returned to Jerusalem. Battles with the Philistines
net.1CH_20_4: Later there was a battle with the Philistines in Gezer. At that time Sibbekai the Hushathite killed Sippai, one of the descendants of the Rephaim, and the Philistines were subdued.
net.1CH_20_5: There was another battle with the Philistines in which Elhanan son of Jair the Bethlehemite killed the brother of Goliath the Gittite, whose spear had a shaft as big as the crossbeam of a weaver’s loom.
net.1CH_20_6: In a battle in Gath there was a large man who had six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot—twenty-four in all! He too was a descendant of Rapha.
net.1CH_20_7: When he taunted Israel, Jonathan son of Shimea, David’s brother, killed him.
net.1CH_20_8: These were the descendants of Rapha who lived in Gath; they were killed by the hand of David and his soldiers.
net.1CH_21_1: An adversary opposed Israel, inciting David to count how many warriors Israel had.
net.1CH_21_2: David told Joab and the leaders of the army, “Go, count the number of warriors from Beer Sheba to Dan. Then bring back a report to me so I may know how many we have.”
net.1CH_21_3: Joab replied, “May the Lord make his army a hundred times larger! My master, O king, do not all of them serve my master? Why does my master want to do this? Why bring judgment on Israel?”
net.1CH_21_4: But the king’s edict stood, despite Joab’s objections. So Joab left and traveled throughout Israel before returning to Jerusalem.
net.1CH_21_5: Joab reported to David the number of warriors. In all Israel there were 1,100,000 sword-wielding soldiers; Judah alone had 470,000 sword-wielding soldiers.
net.1CH_21_6: Now Joab did not number Levi and Benjamin, for the king’s edict disgusted him.
net.1CH_21_7: God was also offended by it, so he attacked Israel.
net.1CH_21_8: David said to God, “I have sinned greatly by doing this! Now, please remove the guilt of your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.”
net.1CH_21_9: The Lord told Gad, David’s prophet,
net.1CH_21_10: “Go, tell David, ‘This is what the Lord says: “I am offering you three forms of judgment from which to choose. Pick one of them.”’”
net.1CH_21_11: Gad went to David and told him, “This is what the Lord says: ‘Pick one of these:
net.1CH_21_12: three years of famine, or three months being chased by your enemies and struck down by their swords, or three days being struck down by the Lord, during which a plague will invade the land and the angel of the Lord will destroy throughout Israel’s territory.’ Now, decide what I should tell the one who sent me.”
net.1CH_21_13: David said to Gad, “I am very upset! I prefer to be attacked by the Lord, for his mercy is very great; I do not want to be attacked by men!”
net.1CH_21_14: So the Lord sent a plague through Israel, and 70,000 Israelite men died.
net.1CH_21_15: God sent an angel to ravage Jerusalem. As he was doing so, the Lord watched and relented from his judgment. He told the angel who was destroying, “That’s enough! Stop now!” Now the angel of the Lord was standing near the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
net.1CH_21_16: David looked up and saw the angel of the Lord standing between the earth and sky with his sword drawn and in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem. David and the leaders, covered with sackcloth, threw themselves down with their faces to the ground.
net.1CH_21_17: David said to God, “Was I not the one who decided to number the army? I am the one who sinned and committed this awful deed! As for these sheep—what have they done? O Lord my God, attack me and my family, but remove the plague from your people!”
net.1CH_21_18: So the angel of the Lord told Gad to instruct David to go up and build an altar for the Lord on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
net.1CH_21_19: So David went up as Gad instructed him to do in the name of the Lord.
net.1CH_21_20: While Ornan was threshing wheat, he turned and saw the messenger, and he and his four sons hid themselves.
net.1CH_21_21: When David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David; he came out from the threshing floor and bowed to David with his face to the ground.
net.1CH_21_22: David said to Ornan, “Sell me the threshing floor so I can build on it an altar for the Lord—I’ll pay top price —so that the plague may be removed from the people.”
net.1CH_21_23: Ornan told David, “You can have it! My master, the king, may do what he wants. Look, I am giving you the oxen for burnt sacrifices, the threshing sledges for wood, and the wheat for an offering. I give it all to you.”
net.1CH_21_24: King David replied to Ornan, “No, I insist on buying it for top price. I will not offer to the Lord what belongs to you or offer a burnt sacrifice that cost me nothing.
net.1CH_21_25: So David bought the place from Ornan for 600 pieces of gold.
net.1CH_21_26: David built there an altar to the Lord and offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings. He called out to the Lord, and the Lord responded by sending fire from the sky and consuming the burnt sacrifice on the altar.
net.1CH_21_27: The Lord ordered the messenger to put his sword back into its sheath.
net.1CH_21_28: At that time, when David saw that the Lord responded to him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he sacrificed there.
net.1CH_21_29: Now the Lord’s tabernacle (which Moses had made in the wilderness) and the altar for burnt sacrifices were at that time at the worship center in Gibeon.
net.1CH_21_30: But David could not go before it to seek God’s will, for he was afraid of the sword of the angel of the Lord.
net.1CH_22_1: David then said, “This is the place where the temple of the Lord God will be, along with the altar for burnt sacrifices for Israel.” David Orders a Temple to Be Built
net.1CH_22_2: David ordered the resident foreigners in the land of Israel to be called together. He appointed some of them to be stonecutters to chisel stones for the building of God’s temple.
net.1CH_22_3: David supplied a large amount of iron for the nails of the doors of the gates and for braces, more bronze than could be weighed,
net.1CH_22_4: and more cedar logs than could be counted. (The Sidonians and Tyrians had brought a large amount of cedar logs to David.)
net.1CH_22_5: David said, “My son Solomon is just an inexperienced young man, and the temple to be built for the Lord must be especially magnificent so it will become famous and be considered splendid by all the nations. Therefore I will make preparations for its construction.” So David made extensive preparations before he died.
net.1CH_22_6: He summoned his son Solomon and charged him to build a temple for the Lord God of Israel.
net.1CH_22_7: David said to Solomon: “My son, I really wanted to build a temple to honor the Lord my God.
net.1CH_22_8: But this was the Lord’s message to me: ‘You have spilled a great deal of blood and fought many battles. You must not build a temple to honor me, for you have spilled a great deal of blood on the ground before me.
net.1CH_22_9: Look, you will have a son, who will be a peaceful man. I will give him rest from all his enemies on every side. Indeed, Solomon will be his name; I will give Israel peace and quiet during his reign.
net.1CH_22_10: He will build a temple to honor me; he will become my son, and I will become his father. I will grant to his dynasty permanent rule over Israel.’
net.1CH_22_11: “Now, my son, may the Lord be with you! May you succeed and build a temple for the Lord your God, just as he announced you would.
net.1CH_22_12: Only may the Lord give you insight and understanding when he places you in charge of Israel, so you may obey the law of the Lord your God.
net.1CH_22_13: Then you will succeed, if you carefully obey the rules and regulations which the Lord ordered Moses to give to Israel. Be strong and brave! Don’t be afraid and don’t panic!
net.1CH_22_14: Now, look, I have made every effort to supply what is needed to build the Lord’s temple. I have stored up 100,000 talents of gold, 1,000,000 talents of silver, and so much bronze and iron it cannot be weighed, as well as wood and stones. Feel free to add more!
net.1CH_22_15: You also have available many workers, including stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and an innumerable array of workers who are skilled
net.1CH_22_16: in using gold, silver, bronze, and iron. Get up and begin the work! May the Lord be with you!”
net.1CH_22_17: David ordered all the officials of Israel to support his son Solomon.
net.1CH_22_18: He told them, “The Lord your God is with you! He has made you secure on every side, for he handed over to me the inhabitants of the region and the region is subdued before the Lord and his people.
net.1CH_22_19: Now seek the Lord your God wholeheartedly and with your entire being! Get up and build the sanctuary of the Lord God! Then you can bring the ark of the Lord’s covenant and the holy items dedicated to God’s service into the temple that is built to honor the Lord.”
net.1CH_23_1: When David was old and approaching the end of his life, he made his son Solomon king over Israel.
net.1CH_23_2: David assembled all the leaders of Israel, along with the priests and the Levites.
net.1CH_23_3: The Levites who were thirty years old and up were counted; there were 38,000 men.
net.1CH_23_4: David said, “Of these, 24,000 are to direct the work of the Lord’s temple; 6,000 are to be officials and judges;
net.1CH_23_5: 4,000 are to be gatekeepers; and 4,000 are to praise the Lord with the instruments I supplied for worship.”
net.1CH_23_6: David divided them into groups corresponding to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
net.1CH_23_7: The Gershonites included Ladan and Shimei.
net.1CH_23_8: The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the oldest, Zetham, and Joel—three in all.
net.1CH_23_9: The sons of Shimei: Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran—three in all. These were the leaders of the family of Ladan.
net.1CH_23_10: The sons of Shimei: Jahath, Zina, Jeush, and Beriah. These were Shimei’s sons—four in all.
net.1CH_23_11: Jahath was the oldest and Zizah the second oldest. Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons, so they were considered one family with one responsibility.
net.1CH_23_12: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel—four in all.
net.1CH_23_13: The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron and his descendants were chosen on a permanent basis to consecrate the most holy items, to offer sacrifices before the Lord, to serve him, and to praise his name.
net.1CH_23_14: The descendants of Moses the man of God were considered Levites.
net.1CH_23_15: The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.
net.1CH_23_16: The son of Gershom: Shebuel the oldest.
net.1CH_23_17: The son of Eliezer was Rehabiah, the oldest. Eliezer had no other sons, but Rehabiah had many descendants.
net.1CH_23_18: The son of Izhar: Shelomith the oldest.
net.1CH_23_19: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the oldest, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
net.1CH_23_20: The sons of Uzziel: Micah the oldest, and Isshiah the second.
net.1CH_23_21: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.
net.1CH_23_22: Eleazar died without having sons; he had only daughters. The sons of Kish, their cousins, married them.
net.1CH_23_23: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth—three in all.
net.1CH_23_24: These were the descendants of Levi according to their families, that is, the leaders of families as counted and individually listed who carried out assigned tasks in the Lord’s temple and were twenty years old and up.
net.1CH_23_25: For David said, “The Lord God of Israel has given his people rest and has permanently settled in Jerusalem.
net.1CH_23_26: So the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the items used in its service.”
net.1CH_23_27: According to David’s final instructions, the Levites twenty years old or older were counted.
net.1CH_23_28: Their job was to help Aaron’s descendants in the service of the Lord’s temple. They were to take care of the courtyards, the rooms, ceremonial purification of all holy items, and other jobs related to the service of God’s temple.
net.1CH_23_29: They also took care of the bread that is displayed, the flour for offerings, the unleavened wafers, the round cakes, the mixing, and all the measuring.
net.1CH_23_30: They also stood in a designated place every morning and offered thanks and praise to the Lord. They also did this in the evening
net.1CH_23_31: and whenever burnt sacrifices were offered to the Lord on the Sabbath and at new moon festivals and assemblies. A designated number were to serve before the Lord regularly in accordance with regulations.
net.1CH_23_32: They were in charge of the meeting tent and the Holy Place, and helped their relatives, the descendants of Aaron, in the service of the Lord’s temple.
net.1CH_24_1: The divisions of Aaron’s descendants were as follows: The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
net.1CH_24_2: Nadab and Abihu died before their father did; they had no sons. Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests.
net.1CH_24_3: David, Zadok (a descendant of Eleazar), and Ahimelech (a descendant of Ithamar) divided them into groups to carry out their assigned responsibilities.
net.1CH_24_4: The descendants of Eleazar had more leaders than the descendants of Ithamar, so they divided them up accordingly; the descendants of Eleazar had sixteen leaders, while the descendants of Ithamar had eight.
net.1CH_24_5: They divided them by lots, for there were officials of the Holy Place and officials designated by God among the descendants of both Eleazar and Ithamar.
net.1CH_24_6: The scribe Shemaiah son of Nethanel, a Levite, wrote down their names before the king, the officials, Zadok the priest, Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the leaders of the priestly and Levite families. One family was drawn by lot from Eleazar, and then the next from Ithamar.
net.1CH_24_7: The first lot went to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
net.1CH_24_8: the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
net.1CH_24_9: the fifth to Malkijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
net.1CH_24_10: the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
net.1CH_24_11: the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
net.1CH_24_12: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
net.1CH_24_13: the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
net.1CH_24_14: the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
net.1CH_24_15: the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Happizzez,
net.1CH_24_16: the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezkel,
net.1CH_24_17: the twenty-first to Jakin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
net.1CH_24_18: the twenty-third to Delaiah, the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.
net.1CH_24_19: This was the order in which they carried out their assigned responsibilities when they entered the Lord’s temple, according to the regulations given them by their ancestor Aaron, just as the Lord God of Israel had instructed him. Remaining Levites
net.1CH_24_20: The rest of the Levites included: Shubael from the sons of Amram, Jehdeiah from the sons of Shubael,
net.1CH_24_21: the firstborn Isshiah from Rehabiah and the sons of Rehabiah,
net.1CH_24_22: Shelomoth from the Izharites, Jahath from the sons of Shelomoth.
net.1CH_24_23: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
net.1CH_24_24: The son of Uzziel: Micah; Shamir from the sons of Micah.
net.1CH_24_25: The brother of Micah: Isshiah. Zechariah from the sons of Isshiah.
net.1CH_24_26: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The son of Jaaziah: Beno.
net.1CH_24_27: The sons of Merari, from Jaaziah: Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
net.1CH_24_28: From Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.
net.1CH_24_30: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the Levites, listed by their families.
net.1CH_24_31: Like their relatives, the descendants of Aaron, they also cast lots before King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, the leaders of families, the priests, and the Levites. The families of the oldest son cast lots along with those of the youngest.
net.1CH_25_1: David and the army officers selected some of the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun to prophesy as they played stringed instruments and cymbals. The following men were assigned this responsibility:
net.1CH_25_2: From the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah. The sons of Asaph were supervised by Asaph, who prophesied under the king’s supervision.
net.1CH_25_3: From the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six in all, under supervision of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied as he played a harp, giving thanks and praise to the Lord.
net.1CH_25_4: From the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-Ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.
net.1CH_25_5: All these were the sons of Heman, the king’s prophet. God had promised him these sons in order to make him prestigious. God gave Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
net.1CH_25_6: All these were under the supervision of their fathers; they were musicians in the Lord’s temple, playing cymbals and stringed instruments as they served in God’s temple. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the supervision of the king.
net.1CH_25_7: They and their relatives, all of them skilled and trained to make music to the Lord, numbered 288.
net.1CH_25_8: They cast lots to determine their responsibilities—oldest as well as youngest, teacher as well as student.
net.1CH_25_9: The first lot went to Asaph’s son Joseph and his relatives and sons—twelve in all, the second to Gedaliah and his relatives and sons—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_10: the third to Zaccur and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_11: the fourth to Izri and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_12: the fifth to Nethaniah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_13: the sixth to Bukkiah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_14: the seventh to Jesharelah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_15: the eighth to Jeshaiah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_16: the ninth to Mattaniah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_17: the tenth to Shimei and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_18: the eleventh to Azarel and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_19: the twelfth to Hashabiah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_20: the thirteenth to Shubael and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_21: the fourteenth to Mattithiah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_22: the fifteenth to Jerimoth and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_23: the sixteenth to Hananiah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_24: the seventeenth to Joshbekashah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_25: the eighteenth to Hanani and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_26: the nineteenth to Mallothi and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_27: the twentieth to Eliathah and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_28: the twenty-first to Hothir and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_29: the twenty-second to Giddalti and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_30: the twenty-third to Mahazioth and his sons and relatives—twelve in all,
net.1CH_25_31: the twenty-fourth to Romamti-Ezer and his sons and relatives—twelve in all.
net.1CH_26_1: The divisions of the gatekeepers: From the Korahites: Meshelemiah, son of Kore, one of the sons of Asaph.
net.1CH_26_2: Meshelemiah’s sons: The firstborn Zechariah, the second Jediael, the third Zebadiah, the fourth Jathniel,
net.1CH_26_3: the fifth Elam, the sixth Jehohanan, and the seventh Elihoenai.
net.1CH_26_4: Obed-Edom’s sons: The firstborn Shemaiah, the second Jehozabad, the third Joah, the fourth Sakar, the fifth Nethanel,
net.1CH_26_5: the sixth Ammiel, the seventh Issachar, and the eighth Peullethai. (Indeed, God blessed Obed-Edom.)
net.1CH_26_6: His son Shemaiah also had sons, who were leaders of their families, for they were highly respected.
net.1CH_26_7: The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad. His relatives Elihu and Semakiah were also respected.
net.1CH_26_8: All these were the descendants of Obed-Edom. They and their sons and relatives were respected men, capable of doing their responsibilities. There were sixty-two of them related to Obed-Edom.
net.1CH_26_9: Meshelemiah had sons and relatives who were respected—eighteen in all.
net.1CH_26_10: Hosah, one of the descendants of Merari, had sons: The firstborn Shimri (he was not actually the firstborn, but his father gave him that status),
net.1CH_26_11: the second Hilkiah, the third Tebaliah, and the fourth Zechariah. All Hosah’s sons and relatives numbered thirteen.
net.1CH_26_12: These divisions of the gatekeepers, corresponding to their leaders, had assigned responsibilities, like their relatives, as they served in the Lord’s temple.
net.1CH_26_13: They cast lots, both young and old, according to their families, to determine which gate they would be responsible for.
net.1CH_26_14: The lot for the east gate went to Shelemiah. They then cast lots for his son Zechariah, a wise adviser, and the lot for the north gate went to him.
net.1CH_26_15: Obed-Edom was assigned the south gate, and his sons were assigned the storehouses.
net.1CH_26_16: Shuppim and Hosah were assigned the west gate, along with the Shalleketh gate on the upper road. One guard was adjacent to another.
net.1CH_26_17: Each day there were six Levites posted on the east, four on the north, and four on the south. At the storehouses they were posted in pairs.
net.1CH_26_18: At the court on the west there were four posted on the road and two at the court.
net.1CH_26_19: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers who were descendants of Korah and Merari. Supervisors of the Storehouses
net.1CH_26_20: Their fellow Levites were in charge of the storehouses in God’s temple and the storehouses containing consecrated items.
net.1CH_26_21: The descendants of Ladan, who were descended from Gershon through Ladan and were leaders of the families of Ladan the Gershonite, included Jehieli
net.1CH_26_22: and the sons of Jehieli, Zetham and his brother Joel. They were in charge of the storehouses in the Lord’s temple.
net.1CH_26_23: As for the Amramites, Izharites, Hebronites, and Uzzielites:
net.1CH_26_24: Shebuel son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was the supervisor of the storehouses.
net.1CH_26_25: His relatives through Eliezer included: Rehabiah his son, Jeshaiah his son, Joram his son, Zikri his son, and Shelomith his son.
net.1CH_26_26: Shelomith and his relatives were in charge of all the storehouses containing the consecrated items dedicated by King David, the family leaders who led units of a thousand and a hundred, and the army officers.
net.1CH_26_27: They had dedicated some of the plunder taken in battles to be used for repairs on the Lord’s temple.
net.1CH_26_28: They were also in charge of everything dedicated by Samuel the prophet, Saul son of Kish, Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah; Shelomith and his relatives were in charge of everything that had been dedicated.
net.1CH_26_29: As for the Izharites: Kenaniah and his sons were given responsibilities outside the temple as officers and judges over Israel.
net.1CH_26_30: As for the Hebronites: Hashabiah and his relatives, 1,700 respected men, were assigned responsibilities in Israel west of the Jordan; they did the Lord’s work and the king’s service.
net.1CH_26_31: As for the Hebronites: Jeriah was the leader of the Hebronites according to the genealogical records. In the fortieth year of David’s reign, they examined the records and discovered there were highly respected men in Jazer in Gilead.
net.1CH_26_32: Jeriah had 2,700 relatives who were respected family leaders. King David placed them in charge of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh; they took care of all matters pertaining to God and the king.
net.1CH_27_1: What follows is a list of Israelite family leaders and commanders of units of a thousand and a hundred, as well as their officers who served the king in various matters. Each division was assigned to serve for one month during the year; each consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_2: Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division, which was assigned the first month. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_3: He was a descendant of Perez; he was in charge of all the army officers for the first month.
net.1CH_27_4: Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division assigned the second month; Mikloth was the next in rank. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_5: The third army commander, assigned the third month, was Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest. He was the leader of his division, which consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_6: Benaiah was the leader of the thirty warriors and his division; his son was Ammizabad.
net.1CH_27_7: The fourth, assigned the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab; his son Zebadiah succeeded him. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_8: The fifth, assigned the fifth month, was the commander Shamhuth the Izrahite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_9: The sixth, assigned the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_10: The seventh, assigned the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, an Ephraimite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_11: The eighth, assigned the eighth month, was Sibbekai the Hushathite, a Zerahite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_12: The ninth, assigned the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, a Benjaminite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_13: The tenth, assigned the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, a Zerahite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_14: The eleventh, assigned the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, an Ephraimite. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_15: The twelfth, assigned the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, a descendant of Othniel. His division consisted of 24,000 troops.
net.1CH_27_16: The officers of the Israelite tribes: Eliezer son of Zikri was the leader of the Reubenites, Shephatiah son of Maacah led the Simeonites,
net.1CH_27_17: Hashabiah son of Kemuel led the Levites, Zadok led the descendants of Aaron,
net.1CH_27_18: Elihu, a brother of David, led Judah, Omri son of Michael led Issachar,
net.1CH_27_19: Ishmaiah son of Obadiah led Zebulun, Jerimoth son of Azriel led Naphtali,
net.1CH_27_20: Hoshea son of Azaziah led the Ephraimites, Joel son of Pedaiah led the half-tribe of Manasseh,
net.1CH_27_21: Iddo son of Zechariah led the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Jaasiel son of Abner led Benjamin,
net.1CH_27_22: Azarel son of Jeroham led Dan. These were the commanders of the Israelite tribes.
net.1CH_27_23: David did not count the males twenty years old and under, for the Lord had promised to make Israel as numerous as the stars in the sky.
net.1CH_27_24: Joab son of Zeruiah started to count the men but did not finish. God was angry with Israel because of this, so the number was not recorded in the scroll called The Annals of King David. Royal Officials
net.1CH_27_25: Azmaveth son of Adiel was in charge of the king’s storehouses; Jonathan son of Uzziah was in charge of the storehouses in the field, in the cities, in the towns, and in the towers.
net.1CH_27_26: Ezri son of Kelub was in charge of the field workers who farmed the land.
net.1CH_27_27: Shimei the Ramathite was in charge of the vineyards; Zabdi the Shiphmite was in charge of the wine stored in the vineyards.
net.1CH_27_28: Baal Hanan the Gederite was in charge of the olive and sycamore trees in the foothills; Joash was in charge of the storehouses of olive oil.
net.1CH_27_29: Shitrai the Sharonite was in charge of the cattle grazing in Sharon; Shaphat son of Adlai was in charge of the cattle in the valleys.
net.1CH_27_30: Obil the Ishmaelite was in charge of the camels; Jehdeiah the Meronothite was in charge of the donkeys.
net.1CH_27_31: Jaziz the Hagrite was in charge of the sheep. All these were the officials in charge of King David’s property.
net.1CH_27_32: Jonathan, David’s uncle, was a wise adviser and scribe; Jehiel son of Hacmoni cared for the king’s sons.
net.1CH_27_33: Ahithophel was the king’s adviser; Hushai the Arkite was the king’s confidant.
net.1CH_27_34: Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah and by Abiathar. Joab was the commanding general of the king’s army.
net.1CH_28_1: David assembled in Jerusalem all the officials of Israel, including the commanders of the tribes, the commanders of the army divisions that served the king, the commanders of units of a thousand and a hundred, the officials who were in charge of all the property and livestock of the king and his sons, the eunuchs, and the warriors, including the most skilled of them.
net.1CH_28_2: King David rose to his feet and said: “Listen to me, my brothers and my people. I wanted to build a temple where the ark of the Lord’s covenant could be placed as a footstool for our God. I have made the preparations for building it.
net.1CH_28_3: But God said to me, ‘You must not build a temple to honor me, for you are a warrior and have spilled blood.’
net.1CH_28_4: The Lord God of Israel chose me out of my father’s entire family to become king over Israel and have a permanent dynasty. Indeed, he chose Judah as leader, and my father’s family within Judah, and then he picked me out from among my father’s sons and made me king over all Israel.
net.1CH_28_5: From all the many sons the Lord has given me, he chose Solomon my son to rule on his behalf over Israel.
net.1CH_28_6: He said to me, ‘Solomon your son is the one who will build my temple and my courts, for I have chosen him to become my son and I will become his father.
net.1CH_28_7: I will establish his kingdom permanently, if he remains committed to obeying my commands and regulations, as you are doing this day.’
net.1CH_28_8: So now, in the sight of all Israel, the Lord’s assembly, and in the hearing of our God, I say this: Carefully observe all the commands of the Lord your God, so that you may possess this good land and may leave it as a permanent inheritance for your children after you.
net.1CH_28_9: “And you, Solomon my son, obey the God of your father and serve him with a submissive attitude and a willing spirit, for the Lord examines all minds and understands every motive of one’s thoughts. If you seek him, he will let you find him, but if you abandon him, he will reject you permanently.
net.1CH_28_10: Realize now that the Lord has chosen you to build a temple as his sanctuary. Be strong and do it!”
net.1CH_28_11: David gave to his son Solomon the blueprints for the temple porch, its buildings, its treasuries, its upper areas, its inner rooms, and the room for atonement.
net.1CH_28_12: He gave him the blueprints of all he envisioned for the courts of the Lord’s temple, all the surrounding rooms, the storehouses of God’s temple, and the storehouses for the holy items.
net.1CH_28_13: He gave him the regulations for the divisions of priests and Levites, for all the assigned responsibilities within the Lord’s temple, and for all the items used in the service of the Lord’s temple.
net.1CH_28_14: He gave him the prescribed weight for all the gold items to be used in various types of service in the Lord’s temple, for all the silver items to be used in various types of service,
net.1CH_28_15: for the gold lampstands and their gold lamps, including the weight of each lampstand and its lamps, for the silver lampstands, including the weight of each lampstand and its lamps, according to the prescribed use of each lampstand,
net.1CH_28_16: for the gold used in the display tables, including the amount to be used in each table, for the silver to be used in the silver tables,
net.1CH_28_17: for the pure gold used for the meat forks, bowls, and jars, for the small gold bowls, including the weight for each bowl, for the small silver bowls, including the weight for each bowl,
net.1CH_28_18: and for the refined gold of the incense altar. He gave him the blueprint for the seat of the gold cherubim that spread their wings and provide shelter for the ark of the Lord’s covenant.
net.1CH_28_19: David said, “All this I put in writing as the Lord directed me and gave me insight regarding the details of the blueprints.”
net.1CH_28_20: David said to his son Solomon: “Be strong and brave! Do it! Don’t be afraid and don’t panic! For the Lord God, my God, is with you. He will not leave you or abandon you before all the work for the service of the Lord’s temple is finished.
net.1CH_28_21: Here are the divisions of the priests and Levites who will perform all the service of God’s temple. All the willing and skilled men are ready to assist you in all the work and perform their service. The officials and all the people are ready to follow your instructions.”
net.1CH_29_1: King David said to the entire assembly: “My son Solomon, the one whom God has chosen, is just an inexperienced young man, and the task is great, for this palace is not for man, but for the Lord God.
net.1CH_29_2: So I have made every effort to provide what is needed for the temple of my God, including the gold, silver, bronze, iron, wood, as well as a large amount of onyx, settings of antimony and other stones, all kinds of precious stones, and alabaster.
net.1CH_29_3: Now, to show my commitment to the temple of my God, I donate my personal treasure of gold and silver to the temple of my God, in addition to all that I have already supplied for this holy temple.
net.1CH_29_4: This includes 3,000 talents of gold from Ophir and 7,000 talents of refined silver for overlaying the walls of the buildings,
net.1CH_29_5: for gold and silver items, and for all the work of the craftsmen. Who else wants to contribute to the Lord today?”
net.1CH_29_6: The leaders of the families, the leaders of the Israelite tribes, the commanders of units of a thousand and a hundred, and the supervisors of the king’s work contributed willingly.
net.1CH_29_7: They donated for the service of God’s temple 5,000 talents and 10,000 darics of gold, 10,000 talents of silver, 18,000 talents of bronze, and 100,000 talents of iron.
net.1CH_29_8: All who possessed precious stones donated them to the treasury of the Lord’s temple, which was under the supervision of Jehiel the Gershonite.
net.1CH_29_9: The people were delighted with their donations, for they contributed to the Lord with a willing attitude; King David was also very happy. David Praises the Lord
net.1CH_29_10: David praised the Lord before the entire assembly: “O Lord God of our father Israel, you deserve praise forevermore!
net.1CH_29_11: O Lord, you are great, mighty, majestic, magnificent, glorious, and sovereign over all the sky and earth! You, Lord, have dominion and exalt yourself as the ruler of all.
net.1CH_29_12: You are the source of wealth and honor; you rule over all. You possess strength and might to magnify and give strength to all.
net.1CH_29_13: Now, our God, we give thanks to you and praise your majestic name!
net.1CH_29_14: “But who am I and who are my people, that we should be in a position to contribute this much? Indeed, everything comes from you, and we have simply given back to you what is yours.
net.1CH_29_15: For we are resident foreigners and temporary settlers in your presence, as all our ancestors were; our days are like a shadow on the earth, without security.
net.1CH_29_16: O Lord our God, all this wealth, which we have collected to build a temple for you to honor your holy name, comes from you; it all belongs to you.
net.1CH_29_17: I know, my God, that you examine thoughts and are pleased with integrity. With pure motives I contribute all this; and now I look with joy as your people who have gathered here contribute to you.
net.1CH_29_18: O Lord God of our ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, always maintain these motives of your people and keep them devoted to you.
net.1CH_29_19: Make my son Solomon willing to obey your commands, rules, and regulations, and to complete building the palace for which I have made preparations.”
net.1CH_29_20: David told the entire assembly: “Praise the Lord your God!” So the entire assembly praised the Lord God of their ancestors; they bowed down and stretched out flat on the ground before the Lord and the king. David Designates Solomon King
net.1CH_29_21: The next day they made sacrifices and offered burnt sacrifices to the Lord (1,000 bulls, 1,000 rams, 1,000 lambs), along with their accompanying drink offerings and many other sacrifices for all Israel.
net.1CH_29_22: They held a feast before the Lord that day and celebrated. Then they designated Solomon, David’s son, as king a second time; before the Lord they anointed him as ruler and Zadok as priest.
net.1CH_29_23: Solomon sat on the Lord’s throne as king in place of his father David; he was successful and all Israel was loyal to him.
net.1CH_29_24: All the officers and warriors, as well as all of King David’s sons, pledged their allegiance to King Solomon.
net.1CH_29_25: The Lord greatly magnified Solomon before all Israel and bestowed on him greater majesty than any king of Israel before him. David’s Reign Comes to an End
net.1CH_29_26: David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
net.1CH_29_27: He reigned over Israel forty years; he reigned in Hebron seven years and in Jerusalem thirty-three years.
net.1CH_29_28: He died at a good old age, having enjoyed long life, wealth, and honor. His son Solomon succeeded him.
net.1CH_29_29: King David’s accomplishments, from start to finish, are recorded in the Annals of Samuel the prophet, the Annals of Nathan the prophet, and the Annals of Gad the prophet.
net.1CH_29_30: Recorded there are all the facts about his reign and accomplishments, and an account of the events that involved him, Israel, and all the neighboring kingdoms.
